blocks:
Ayahs

أَتَىٰٓ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ فَلَا تَسْتَعْجِلُوهُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۱)
Allah’s edict is coming! So do not seek to hasten it. Immaculate is He and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (1)
يُنَزِّلُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ بِٱلرُّوحِ مِنْ أَمْرِهِۦ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦٓ أَنْ أَنذِرُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱتَّقُونِ (۲)
He sends down the angels with the spirit of His command to whomever He wishes of His servants: ‘Warn [the people] that there is no god except Me; so be wary of Me.’ (2)

Key Points

Although the context of the Ayah concerns the haste of the disbelievers regarding the descent of Allah's wrath, the phrase "اتى امراللَّه" (Allah's command has come) is not exclusive to the command of Divine wrath. Rather, it includes all of Allah's commands, such as the command for Jihad, the command for the appearance of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, and the command for the establishment of the Day of Resurrection, and one should not hasten these matters.

The "روح" (Spirit/Ruh) is one of the close, favored angels of Allah, whose name appears separately in the Quran, usually alongside "الملائكة" (the angels): "یوم یقوم الروح و الملائكة" (1), "تعرج الملائكة و الرّوح الیه" (2), and "تنزّل الملائكة والروح". However, here, in the Ayah "ینّزل الملائكة بالرّوح" (He sends down the angels with the Spirit), the letter 'باء' (bā') is used instead of the conjunction 'واو' (wāw) between the two words. Perhaps this is because the intent of "روح" in this Ayah is not that angel, but rather the literal meaning of "روح," which is spiritual life. In this case, the meaning of the Ayah becomes: Allah sends down the angels, accompanied by the means of life, upon whichever of His servants He wills. Just as in the Ayah "وكذلك اوحینا الیك روحاً من امرنا" (3), the intent of "روح" is the Quran, which is the source of spiritual life: "دعاكم لما یحییكم" (4).

1) Naba', 37. 2) Ma'arij, 4. 3) Shura, 52. 4) Anfal, 24.

Messages

1- Divine commands are definite, and the occurrence of His wrath is certain. "اَتى امر اللَّه" (Allah's command has come); 'اَتى' is a past tense verb, implying that the punishment has already arrived.

2- Allah fulfills His promises. "اَتى امر اللَّه". In Ayah 109 of Surah Al-Baqarah and Ayah 24 of Surah At-Tawbah, Allah had promised: "حتى یأتى اللَّه بامره" (until Allah brings His command). Now He says: "اتى امراللَّه" (Allah's command has come).

3- Do not hasten Allah's work, for His work is based on wisdom and is accomplished at its proper time. "فلا تستعجلوه" (so do not hasten Him).

4- The descent of the Angel of Revelation, the content of the revelation, and the one who receives it—all are within the sphere of Divine Will. "من امره" (by His command).

5- Prophethood (Risalah) is a matter of appointment, not acquisition. "من یشاء" (whomever He wills). Of course, Allah is Wise and does not grant the station of prophethood to anyone without reason: "اللَّه اعلم حیث یجعل رسالته" (5).

6- The first condition for receiving Divine revelation has been the spirit of servitude and worship (عبودیّت) of the prophets. "من عباده" (upon His servants).

7- Warning the people is at the forefront of the prophets' duties. "انذروا" (warn).

8- The foundation of beliefs is Monotheism (Tawhid), and the foundation of righteous action is piety (Taqwa). "لااله الاّ انا فاتّقون" (There is no god except Me, so fear Me).

9- Piety and purity gain meaning under the shadow of Monotheism. "لااله الاّ انا فاتّقون" (There is no god except Me, so fear Me).

5) An'am, 124.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah An-Nahl

The sixteenth Surah of the Holy Quran, which has 128 Ayahs, is named "An-Nahl" (The Bee) because it refers to the creation of the honeybee. Although the Quran is the book of legislation (Tashri'), the names of many of its Surahs are based on creation (Takwin), such as "نَجْم = Star," "شَمْس = Sun," "فیل = Elephant," "عنكبوت = Spider," and "نحل = Bee." These names symbolize that all beings in existence, whether in the heavens or on earth, whether small or large, are equal before His power, and the book of Sharia (Law) is founded upon the book of nature, and these two books originate from a single Source.

One of the names of this Surah is the Surah of Blessings (Ni'mah), because more than fifty blessings are mentioned within it. This Surah discusses Divine blessings, proofs of Monotheism and the Resurrection, rulings on Jihad, warnings to the polytheists, prohibition of oppression and indecency (fahsha), breaking covenants, innovations (bid'ah), and satanic whispers.

According to the late Allamah Tabataba'i in Tafsir al-Mizan, it is understood from the occasions of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayahs of this Surah that its first forty Ayahs relate to the late period in Mecca, and the other eighty-eight Ayahs relate to the early period of the migration (Hijra) to Medina.

Ayahs

خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ تَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۳)
He created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom. He is above having any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (3)
خَلَقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ فَإِذَا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ (٤)
He created man from a drop of [seminal] fluid, and, behold, he is an open contender! (4)

Key Points

Ayah 4 of this Surah is similar to Ayah 77 of Surah Ya Sin, which says: «خلقناه من نطفة فاذا هو خصیم مبین و ضرب لنا مثلاً و نسى خلقه» meaning that although We created him from a drop of fluid (nutfah), yet he has become so arrogant that he rises up in enmity against Us and sets forth this example for Us—how decayed bones can be brought back to life—as if he has forgotten his own creation.

Messages

1- The creation of the heavens and the earth is not vain or false, but is established upon truth. «خلق... بالحقّ»

2- Which idol or deity can be a partner to Allah in creation? «خلقِ... تعالى عمّا یشركون»

3- The struggle against polytheism (Shirk) must be continuous and persistent. The phrase «تعالى عمّا یشركون» is repeated in the first and third Ayahs.

4- Human arrogance and pride reach such an extent that man engages in hostility and expresses enmity toward his Creator. «خصیم مبین»

Ayahs

وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمَ خَلَقَهَا ۗ لَكُمْ فِيهَا دِفْءٌ وَمَنَـٰفِعُ وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (٥)
He created the cattle, in which there is warmth for you and [other] uses, and some of them you eat. (5)
وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا جَمَالٌ حِينَ تُرِيحُونَ وَحِينَ تَسْرَحُونَ (٦)
There is in them a beauty for you when you bring them home for rest and when you drive them forth to pasture. (6)

Key Points

Tasraḥūn (تَسْرَحُونَ) is derived from sarḥ (سَرْح), meaning sending livestock out to pasture, and tarīḥūn (تَرِيحُونَ) is derived from rawḥ (رَوْح), meaning the time when the livestock return to the fold. The benefits of animals for humans are numerous. Their milk and meat are for food, their hide and wool are for shoes and clothing, their backs are for carrying loads, their feet are for plowing, and even their waste is for fertilizer. Despite all these blessings, the effort required from humans is very little. In narrations, animal husbandry is mentioned as the best occupation after agriculture, provided that it is accompanied by the payment of Zakat and attention to the deprived.

Messages

1- Livestock (like other creatures) have been created for mankind. «وَالْأَنْعَامَ خَلَقَهَا لَكُمْ»

2- Paying attention to blessings revives the love for the Creator and the spirit of servitude in man. «وَالْأَنْعَامَ خَلَقَهَا لَكُمْ»

3- Vegetarianism is not a virtue. Allah counts the eating of animal meat as one of their benefits. «وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ»

4- The beauty (jamāl) of a society lies in independence, self-sufficiency, production, and the development of animal husbandry. «جَمَالٌ حِينَ...»

5- Beauty (jamāl) and adornment are among the natural needs of the individual and society. «لَكُمْ فِيهَا جَمَالٌ»

6- The beauty of society lies in movement and effort, not stagnation and lethargy—and that movement should be collective, not individualistic! «فِيهَا جَمَالٌ حِينَ تُرِيحُونَ»

7- Beauty lies in serving the people, not just thinking about filling one's own stomach. Beauty lies in being under the command of a wise shepherd, not being abandoned and aimless. «جَمَالٌ حِينَ تُرِيحُونَ وَ...»

Ayah

وَتَحْمِلُ أَثْقَالَكُمْ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ لَّمْ تَكُونُوا۟ بَـٰلِغِيهِ إِلَّا بِشِقِّ ٱلْأَنفُسِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۷)
And they bear your burdens to towns, which you could not reach except by straining yourselves. Indeed your Lord is most kind and merciful. (7)

Messages

1- Although livestock are stronger, by Divine power, they are subservient to humans. «و تحمل اثقالكم»

2- Livestock are a source of human welfare in life. «الاّ بشقّ الانفس» (So that the value of the blessings becomes apparent, let us assume what would happen if these animals did not exist?)

3- The Divine blessings upon humanity are based on His grace and mercy, not because we have any claim upon Him. «انّ ربّكم لرؤف رحيم»

Ayah

وَٱلْخَيْلَ وَٱلْبِغَالَ وَٱلْحَمِيرَ لِتَرْكَبُوهَا وَزِينَةً ۚ وَيَخْلُقُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۸)
And horses, mules and asses, for you to ride them, and for pomp, and He creates what you do not know. (8)

Key Points

The word "Khayl" (خیل) means arrogance, but here it refers to "horses." It is as if riding a horse gives a person a feeling of greatness and arrogance. The word "Bighal" (بغال) means "mules," which are produced by the mating of a horse and a donkey. The word "Humur" (حمیر) is the plural of "Himar" (حمار), meaning "donkey."

Messages

  1. The transportation of goods and passengers is among the primary needs of humanity, for which Allah has created animals. «والخیل والبغال و الحمیر لتركبوها»

  2. Riding mounts provides comfort and ease, and is also considered a type of adornment and beauty. «لتركبوها و زینة»

  3. Adornment (Zinah) is one of the innate needs of humanity. «زینة»

  4. A mount must primarily be for riding, and secondarily a source of adornment. «لتركبوها و زینة» (This is contrary to some people who seek to acquire vehicles solely for luxury and ceremony, rather than having a genuine need for them.)

  5. Allah's hand is open in creation (His creative power is vast). «و یخلق ما لاتعلمون»

  6. Modern means of transportation—whether cars, trains, or airplanes—are, in reality, creatures of Allah, not man. «و یخلق ما لاتعلمون»

Ayah

وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ قَصْدُ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَمِنْهَا جَآئِرٌ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ لَهَدَىٰكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۹)
With Allah rests guidance to the straight path, and some of the paths are devious, and had He wished He would have guided you all. (9)

Key Points

Among the matters that Allah has made incumbent upon Himself is the guidance and instruction of people. As He says: «إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا لَلْهُدَى» (6) and in this Ayah, He says: «عَلَى اللَّهِ قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ». Qasd (قصد) means moderation and temperance, and the meaning of «قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ» is the middle path, meaning the straight path. Just as Luqman advises his son thus: «وَاقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ» (7), meaning: Be moderate and temperate in your walking.

6) Layl, 12. 7) Luqman, 19.

Messages

1- Alongside the material blessings (mentioned in the previous verses), we should pay attention to the spiritual blessings, the most important of which is guidance. «وَعَلَى اللَّهِ قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ»

2- Allah only shows the straight path; the deviant paths originate from ourselves. «وَعَلَى اللَّهِ قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ وَمِنْهَا جَائِرٌ»

3- Allah did not intend for people to believe by compulsion. Therefore, the deviation of a group of people is not a sign of their overcoming the Will of Allah or the inability of the Lord to guide them. «لَوْ شَاءَ لَهَدَاكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ»

4- Allah has left man free in choosing his path. «لَوْ شَاءَ لَهَدَاكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً ۖ لَّكُم مِّنْهُ شَرَابٌ وَمِنْهُ شَجَرٌ فِيهِ تُسِيمُونَ (۱٠)
It is He who sends down water from the sky: from it you get your drink and with it are [sustained] the plants wherein you pasture your herds. (10)

Key Points

The word "تَسِیمُونَ" (tasīmūn) is derived from "إِسَامَة" (isāmah), meaning the grazing of animals in a pasture. The word "شَجَر" (shajar) in the Arabic language has a general meaning that includes every type of plant, whether a tree or a bush. Just as in Ayah 146 of Surah As-Saffat, concerning the gourd, He says: "شَجَرَةٍ مِّن يَقْطِينٍ" (a tree of gourd), even though the gourd has a vine/bush, not a tree.

Messages

1- The descent of rain is not accidental; it is by the will of Allah. "هُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ" (It is He Who sent down).

2- The descent of rain is based on the benefit of mankind. "لَكُم" (for you).

3- From one water source, humans, plants, and animals benefit. "مِنْهُ شَرَابٌ وَمِنْهُ شَجَرٌ..." (from it is drink, and from it is herbage...).

Ayah

يُنۢبِتُ لَكُم بِهِ ٱلزَّرْعَ وَٱلزَّيْتُونَ وَٱلنَّخِيلَ وَٱلْأَعْنَـٰبَ وَمِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۱۱)
For you He makes the crops grow with it and olives, date palms, vines, and fruits of all kinds. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who reflect. (11)

Key Points

Among the various types of plants and fruits, Allah has mentioned the olive, the date, and the grape in this Ayah. Nutrition specialists say: Few fruits are as beneficial and necessary for the body as these are.(8) In Ayah 35 of Surah An-Noor, the Quran refers to the olive as a "شجرة مباركة" (blessed tree) and mentions its oil.

8) Volume seven of the book "The First University and the Last Prophet" discusses the properties of grapes and dates.

Messages

1- Causing growth is the work of Allah, not the farmer. "ینبت لكم"

2- Various types of plants, trees, and fruits have been created for mankind. "لكم"

3- Products and fruits are waypoints, not stopping points; they are signs and markers on the path, not the destination. "لآیة"

4- Seeing and knowing is not enough; thinking and contemplating existence is necessary. "لقوم یتفكرون"

Ayah

وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ وَٱلنُّجُومُ مُسَخَّرَٰتٌۢ بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (۱۲)
He disposed the night and the day for you, and the sun, the moon and the stars are disposed by His command. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason. (12)

Key Points

The meaning of the subjugation of the sun and the moon is the benefit man derives from them. Otherwise, how can a human who cannot create a fly subjugate the sun? Allah, out of His grace, has placed them in the service of mankind.

Messages

1- Existence, both the heavens and the earth, has been created for man. «سخّر لكم ...»

2- Existence is under the restraint and control of Allah. «مسخرّات بامره»

3- The yielding of benefit by existence is by the command of Allah. «بامره»

4- The system of creation is a classroom of Monotheism (Tawhid). «انّ فى ذلك»

5- The system of existence is a ground for growth and attention only for people of thought and intellect, not for the simple-minded or ordinary individuals. «لقوم یعقلون»

Ayah

وَمَا ذَرَأَ لَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهُۥٓ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (۱۳)
And [He disposed for your benefit] whatever He has created for you in the earth of diverse hues—there is indeed a sign in that for a people who take admonition. (13)

Messages

1- Allah has created various and diverse colors for mankind. «ذرأ لكم... مختلفاً الوانه»

2- The diversity of colors in creation is a sign of Allah's power and wisdom. «لآیة»

The more diverse the products of a factory are, the greater the sign of the maker's innovation and creativity.

3- The difference in colors is one of the divine blessings for identifying similar individuals and products. «لآیة لقوم یذّكّرون»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى سَخَّرَ ٱلْبَحْرَ لِتَأْكُلُوا۟ مِنْهُ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُوا۟ مِنْهُ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ مَوَاخِرَ فِيهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۱٤)
It is He who disposed the sea [for your benefit] that you may eat from it fresh meat, and obtain from it ornaments which you wear, and you see the ships plowing through it, that you may seek His bounty and that you may give thanks. (14)

Key Points

| "Mawākhir" (مواخر) is the plural of "mi'kharah" (مِأخرة), derived from "makhr" (مَخر), meaning to cleave or split from the left and right. The role of the sea in human life is immense. Its water is the source of vapor, clouds, and rain. Its depth provides food for humanity with the most delicious fish, and its surface is the cheapest and most extensive route for the transport of goods and passengers. All of this is solely due to Divine planning and power, and humanity has no role in it.

Messages

1- The seas, despite all their grandeur and turbulence, are subdued and placed in the service of humanity. «سخّر البحر»

2- The sea is the most important source for providing fresh and healthy meat. «لحماً طریّاً»

3- Allah has provided not only for humanity's primary needs, such as water and food, but also for their adornment. «حلیةً تلبسونها»

4- The sea gifts humanity with the best natural adornments. «حلیة تلبسونها»

5- The freshness of meat is a value. «لحماً طریّاً»

6- Although the effort to obtain food comes from humanity, the provision (rizq) is from Allah. «لتبتغوا من فضله»

7- Successes and enjoyments must be purposeful. «لعلكم تشكرون»

Ayah

وَأَلْقَىٰ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ رَوَٰسِىَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ وَأَنْهَـٰرًا وَسُبُلًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (۱٥)
He cast firm mountains in the earth lest it should shake with you, and [made] streams and ways, so that you may be guided (15)

Key Points

Mountains are the source of balance and tranquility for the Earth. "Mayd" (مَیْد) means movement to the right and left, and agitation. The phrase "أن تمید بكم" means that mountains are the cause of your tranquility and prevent the Earth from shaking. Imam Ali عليه السلام said regarding the mountains: Allah stabilized the movement of the Earth with large rocks and firm mountains. (9) The roots (base) of the mountains are embedded throughout it and have penetrated its crevices. Just as the agitation of the Earth requires deep and firm mountains, the agitation of the inhabitants of the Earth also requires firm and godly individuals to become a source of tranquility for society. As mentioned in narrations: "جعلهم اللَّه اركان الارض ان تمید باهلها" (Allah made them [the Ahl al-Bayt] the pillars of the Earth lest it should shake with its inhabitants) (10). Allah made the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام the pillars of the Earth so that they might save the inhabitants of the Earth from agitation. Mountains play a major role in human life. By storing winter snow on their slopes, they become the source of spring rivers and streams, and with their various shapes, they serve as road signs, guiding travelers. We understand this point when we imagine that the entire Earth was flat and smooth.

9) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 91. 10) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Mountains are not the product of blind chance, but are based on wise planning. "اَلقى فى الارض رواسى"

2- Because mountains are fixed and firm, they can prevent agitation and be a source of tranquility for the Earth and its inhabitants. "رواسى ان تمید بكم" (Indeed, only someone who is steadfast and firm himself can prevent unrest and agitation in society.)

3- Mountains, with the slopes of their sides, cause water to flow in rivers. "انهاراً"

4- Mountains, contrary to what might seem, are not obstacles to the path, but guides for the path. "سبلاً لعلّكم تهتدون"

5- Mountains are a source of both physical and spiritual guidance. "لعلّكم تهتدون" (Mountains are the best road signs in the desert, and also the best way to know Allah and pay attention to the greatness and power of the Creator.)

Ayah

وَعَلَـٰمَـٰتٍ ۚ وَبِٱلنَّجْمِ هُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (۱٦)
—and the landmarks [as well]—and by the stars they are guided. (16)

Key Points

For movement in deserts and finding paths, we need signs. Natural signs during the day, and stars at night, which Allah refers to in this Ayah. Not only do we need signs in the deserts to distinguish the path from the wrong way, but we also need clear signs to recognize the truth (Haqq) from falsehood (Batil) amidst desires, instincts, and tyrants (Taghuts). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله established signs for after his time, so that whenever people became confused and bewildered, they could look to them and recognize the truth. The first of these is Hadrat Zahra عليها السلام, about whom he said: Her satisfaction is My satisfaction, and Her anger is My anger. Another sign is Abu Dharr al-Ghifari, about whom the Prophet said: The sky has not shaded anyone more truthful than Abu Dharr, so that people may see whom Abu Dharr's tongue supports and in whose place of exile he dies. Another sign is Ammar ibn Yasir, about whom the Prophet said: The killer of Ammar is a deviant group. People saw that in the Battle of Siffin, Ammar was killed by Mu'awiyah's army. And the most important clear sign after the Prophet is Imam Husayn عليه السلام, about whom the Prophet said: "Husayn is from Me, and I am from Husayn." Imam Sadiq and Imam Rida عليهما السلام also say: "نحن العلامات و النّجم رسول اللّه" (We are the signs, and the star is the Messenger of Allah.)(11) Stars are both a means of recognizing the direction of the Qibla and the best means of finding the way in seas and deserts where there are no other signs.

11) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Namuneh; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 207, Amali Mufid, 102.

Messages

1- Could Allah, Who has established signs for the worldly guidance of people, have been neglectful of their spiritual guidance? "و علامات ... یهتدون"

2- The science of astronomy and stargazing is given attention in Islam. "وبالنّجم هم یهتدون"

Ayah

أَفَمَن يَخْلُقُ كَمَن لَّا يَخْلُقُ ۗ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۱۷)
Is He who creates like one who does not create? Will you not then take admonition? (17)

Key Points

From the beginning of Surah An-Nahl until this point, in fifteen verses, He enumerates the divine blessings. In this verse, He draws a general conclusion and expresses this point in the form of a question: Is the One who creates like the idols or the tyrants (taghuts) who do not have the power to create? Why do you turn to them instead of the Exalted God (Allah)?

Messages

1- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), move from the specific to the general. First, state examples, and then draw a general conclusion. After mentioning blessings in 15 verses, He says: «اَفمن یخلق كمن لایخلق»

2- In the method of propagation, call upon the conscience of the audience to judge, and awaken their intellect and innate nature (Fitrah). «افلا تذكرون»

3- Intellect and Revelation are in harmony with each other, and the intellect accepts what the Quran states. «افلا تذكرون»

4- Creation has not ended; rather, it is continuous. «یخلق»

5- Knowledge of God (Allah) and worship of Him are inherent in the nature of all human beings; they only require remembrance (Tadhakkur). «افلا تذكرون»

Ayah

وَإِن تَعُدُّوا۟ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۸)
If you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you will not be able to count them. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful, (18)

Key Points

A similar Ayah appears in Surah Ibrahim, Ayah 33, but there, at the end of the Ayah, He says: «انّ الانسان لظلوم كفّار» (Indeed, man is unjust and ungrateful), while here, at the end of the Ayah, He says: «انّ اللَّه لغفور رحیم» (Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful). Yes! Blessings are a sign of Allah's grace and mercy, but it is man who commits ingratitude (kufrān) and wrongs himself and society.

Although counting the divine blessings is impossible, mentioning and remembering them is necessary. Just as He says in Ayah 11 of Surah Duha: «و امّا بنعمة ربّك فحدّث» (But as for the favor of your Lord, report [it]), and in Ziyarat Amin Allah we read: "ذاكرة لسوابغ آلائك" (Remembering Your abundant favors).

Man does not even have the power to count the divine blessings, let alone the power to thank Him for them.

Who can, with hand and tongue, Fulfill the duty of His gratitude? It is better for the servant to bring An excuse for his own shortcomings to the threshold of God, Otherwise, no one can render What is worthy of His Divinity.

Messages

1- Be thankful for what you know; regarding what you do not know, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful. «انّ اللَّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُسِرُّونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ (۱۹)
and Allah knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose. (19)

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge regarding all matters, whether manifest or hidden, is uniform. «ما تسرّون و ما تعلنون»

2- If we know that Allah is aware of our deeds, we will practice piety (Taqwa). «واللَّه یعلم»

3- Allah is also aware of our intentions and goals. «یعلم ما تسرّون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ لَا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ (۲٠)
Those whom they invoke besides Allah do not create anything and are themselves created. (20)
أَمْوَٰتٌ غَيْرُ أَحْيَآءٍ ۖ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ (۲۱)
They are dead and lifeless, and are not aware when they will be resurrected. (21)

Key Points

Since idolaters used to make idols in the shape of humans and, in their imagination, treated them as conscious beings, Allah therefore called the idols amwāt (dead) in this Ayah, a term used for beings possessing life and consciousness. Repetition of the point is necessary for the ignorant person. Next to amwāt (dead), the phrase ghayr aḥyā’ (not living) is mentioned, even though it conveys the same meaning.

Messages

1- Idols possess neither power, nor life, nor knowledge and consciousness. (While the condition for worship is knowledge, power, and life.) «لایخلقون، اموات، مایشعرون»

2- Whatever the objects of worship besides Allah may be, they are dead. «اموات غیر احیاء»

3- On the Day of Judgment, even the idols will be resurrected. «ما یشعرون ایّان یبعثون»

As He says in Ayah 98 of Surah Al-Anbiya: «انكم و ما تعبدون من دون اللَّه حصب جهنّم»

Ayah

إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۚ فَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ قُلُوبُهُم مُّنكِرَةٌ وَهُم مُّسْتَكْبِرُونَ (۲۲)
Your God is the One God. Those who do not believe in the Hereafter, their hearts are in denial [of the truth], and they are arrogant. (22)

Key Points

The previous verses discussed the inability of beings other than Allah regarding creation and their ignorance of the future. This verse states that your true object of worship is the One God. Kibr (كبر) means considering oneself great. Takabbur (تكبّر) means acting upon the effects of kibr. And Istikbar (استكبار) means that one does not possess greatness inherently, but seeks to create and prove his greatness through various means. It is narrated in traditions that Imam Husayn عليه السلام passed by some poor people who were eating. They invited the Imam to join their meal. The Imam sat beside them and began eating, and then said: Allah does not love the arrogant (or the proud/the mustakbirūn).(12)

(12) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is, in reality, denying the Origin (Mabda'). Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection are interconnected. «الهكم اله واحد فالذین لایؤمنون بالاخرة»
  2. The source of disbelief (kufr) is arrogance (kibr), not ignorance. It is the denial of the truth, not ignorance of the truth. «قلوبهم منكرة و هم مستكبرون»
  3. Belief in the Hereafter removes pride (takabbur) and arrogance (istikbar). «فالذین لایؤمنون ...قلوبهم منكرة»

Ayah

لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُسْتَكْبِرِينَ (۲۳)
Undoubtedly, Allah knows whatever they hide and whatever they disclose. Indeed, He does not like the arrogant. (23)

Key Points

The word jarm (جَرم) means cutting or picking fruit from a tree. Lājarm (لاجَرم) means that the matter is not subject to cutting off, doubt, or suspicion; rather, it is definite and certain.

This Ayah is both a threat to the disbelievers—that Allah is aware of your deeds—and a glad tiding to the believers—that Allah is aware of the situation of your enemies and is capable of punishing them.

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُم مَّاذَآ أَنزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ ۙ قَالُوٓا۟ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۲٤)
When they are asked, ‘What is it that your Lord has sent down?’ They say, ‘Myths of the ancients,’ (24)

Key Points

The word 'Asātīr' (اساطیر) is either the plural of 'Ustūrah' (اُسطوره), meaning mythical stories and superstitious legends that have been written down, or the plural of 'Astar' (اَسطار), meaning lines copied from previous books. This word is quoted nine times in the Qur'an from the mouths of the disbelievers, and in all instances, it is accompanied by the word 'Awwalīn' (اوّلین - the ancients). That is, they used to say: these words are not new; rather, they are merely the fabrications of the ancients. Some sociologists today also consider religion to be the product of ignorance and superstition. This assertion might be due to certain superstitions that have infiltrated the genuine principles of the divine religions, but which, in reality, have no connection to religion itself.

Messages

1- Sending the Prophets and the heavenly Books is among the functions of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) for the guidance and training of people. «انزل ربّكم»

2- The nature of arrogant individuals is to belittle others: sometimes belittling the school of thought (the religion), sometimes the leader, and sometimes the community. «انّ اللَّه لایحب المستكبرین و اذا قیل لهم...»

3- Although only a portion of the Qur'an consists of stories, not all of it, those very stories relate to nations and Prophets about whom the people contemporary to the Prophet were mostly unaware. How, then, can the Qur'an be attributed with: «اساطیر الاولین» (tales of the ancients)?

Ayah

لِيَحْمِلُوٓا۟ أَوْزَارَهُمْ كَامِلَةً يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۙ وَمِنْ أَوْزَارِ ٱلَّذِينَ يُضِلُّونَهُم بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ أَلَا سَآءَ مَا يَزِرُونَ (۲٥)
[with the result] that they will bear the full weight of their own burden on the Day of Resurrection, along with part of the burden of those whom they mislead without any knowledge. Behold! Evil is what they bear! (25)

Key Points

This Ayah concerns the leaders of disbelief (kufr) who, through their false propaganda in this world, cause people to go astray. Therefore, on the Day of Resurrection, they will bear the burden of their own sin of disbelief and the sin of those whom they caused to deviate. As we read in narrations (riwayat): Whoever charts a path of deviation shares in the punishment of all those who deviate, and whoever charts a good path is also given the reward of those who follow that path, without anything being diminished from his share.

Messages

  1. The leaders of disbelief and misguidance will not receive any reduction in punishment. «لیحملوا اوزارهم كاملة»

  2. Whoever invites others to sin is also a partner in their punishment. «من اوزار الّذین یضلّونهم»

  3. The leaders of disbelief bear their share of sin completely, but they bear the share of their followers' sins to the extent that they played a role in them. «كاملة ... و من اوزار»

  4. The root of many deviations is ignorance, and the enemy uses people's ignorance to lead them astray. «یضلّونهم بغیر علم»

  5. Sin is a burden, and what a terrible burden it is. «ساء ما یزرون»

Ayah

قَدْ مَكَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتَى ٱللَّهُ بُنْيَـٰنَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْقَوَاعِدِ فَخَرَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلسَّقْفُ مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَأَتَىٰهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۲٦)
Those who were before them [had also] schemed. Then Allah came at their edifice from the foundations and the roof fell down upon them from above and the punishment overtook them whence they were not aware. (26)

Key Points

This Ayah is both a threat to the plotters and a consolation for the Prophet.

Messages

1- Plotting against the truth has always existed. «قد مكر الذّین من قبلهم»

2- Where the foundation of religion is in danger, Allah Himself intervenes. «فاتى اللَّه»

3- Opponents of religion and the school of thought should know that Allah is against them. «فاتى اللَّه بنیانهم»

4- Wherever the foundation of religion is endangered (in the previous Ayahs, the disbelievers described revelation as myths), the confrontation must also be foundational. «فاتى اللَّه بنیانهم»

5- Dealing with the enemy must be foundational, not superficial or apparent, so that all their intellectual and organizational structures are destroyed. «فاتى اللَّه بنیانهم ... فخرّ علیهم السّقف»

6- Divine punishments are not exclusive to the Hereafter. «فاتى اللَّه بنیانهم»

7- Sometimes a strong structure, instead of protecting a person's life, becomes that person's own grave. «فخرّ علیهم السقف»

8- The time and place of Allah's wrath are unpredictable. «من حیث لایشعرون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يُخْزِيهِمْ وَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآءِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تُشَـٰٓقُّونَ فِيهِمْ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ إِنَّ ٱلْخِزْىَ ٱلْيَوْمَ وَٱلسُّوٓءَ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲۷)
Then, on the Day of Resurrection, He will disgrace them, and say, ‘Where are My “partners” for whose sake you used to defy [Allah]?’ Those who were given knowledge will say, ‘Indeed today disgrace and distress pursue the faithless.’ (27)

Key Points

In the culture of the Quran, knowledge ('ilm) and ignorance (jahl) have a broader meaning than merely knowing and not knowing. The knowledgeable person ('alim) is one whose thought and action are based on truth and reality, even if he does not possess the ability to read and write. And the ignorant person (jahil) is one whose thought and action are based on falsehood, even if he knows all the sciences. The Quran considers the ideology of polytheism (shirk) and idol worship a sign of ignorance, just as it considers ignorance to be the root of the vile act of the people of Lot. In this Ayah too, "اوتوا العلم" (those who were given knowledge) are those who stand against disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk). This means that true knowledge leads man to monotheism (Tawhid) and faith.

Messages

1- The main punishment for criminals is in the Hereafter, and destruction in this world is merely a prelude to it. "ثم یوم القیمة یخزیهم" (Then on the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them).

2- Those who humiliated the believers in this world will themselves be humiliated on the Day of Resurrection. "یخزیهم" (He will disgrace them).

3- Whoever you follow, you must be accountable for it on the Day of Resurrection. "اَین شركائى" (Where are My partners?).

4- In the court of the Day of Resurrection, the polytheists have nothing to say. God's question, "این شركائى" (Where are My partners?), has no answer.

5- True knowledge, which leads to faith and action, is a Divine gift. "اوتوا العلم" (those who were given knowledge).

Ayahs

ٱلَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّىٰهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ ظَالِمِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ فَأَلْقَوُا۟ ٱلسَّلَمَ مَا كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ مِن سُوٓءٍۭ ۚ بَلَىٰٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۸)
—Those whom the angels take away while they were wronging themselves. Thereat they submit: ‘We were not doing any evil!’ ‘Yes, [the angels say to them,] indeed Allah knows best what you used to do! (28)
فَٱدْخُلُوٓا۟ أَبْوَٰبَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَلَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ (۲۹)
Enter the gates of hell to remain in it [forever]. Evil is the [final] abode of the arrogant.’ (29)

Key Points

When the disbelievers are dying, and the angels take their souls, they both declare Islam and submission, and deny their evil past. However, neither does that faith hold value, because it stems from compulsion, not free will, nor is this denial accepted, for Allah is aware of their deeds.

Messages

  1. Death is not annihilation, but rather the seizing of the soul and the separation of the soul from the body. «تتوفیهم»

  2. It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to carry out affairs through intermediaries. «تتوفیهم الملائكة»

  3. Disbelief and polytheism are oppression against one's own humanity. «ظالمى انفسهم»

  4. Man will one day be forced to submit, but what good is it? «فالقوا السّلم ... فادخلوا ابواب جهنّم»

  5. The moment of death is a critical moment for the believer and the disbeliever. Regarding the disbeliever, He says: «تتوفیهم الملائكة ظالمى انفسهم» and regarding the believer, in Ayah 32 of An-Nahl, He says: «تتوفیهم الملائكة طیبین»

  6. Hell (Jahannam) has multiple gates, and every transgressor enters the Fire through a specific path. «ابواب»

  7. The main root of disbelief is the spirit of arrogance. «الكافرین ...المتكبرین»

Ayah

۞ وَقِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ مَاذَآ أَنزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ ۚ قَالُوا۟ خَيْرًا ۗ لِّلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ ۚ وَلَدَارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ ۚ وَلَنِعْمَ دَارُ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۳٠)
But those who were Godwary will be asked, ‘What is it that your Lord has sent down?’ They will say, ‘Good.’ For those who do good in this world there will be a good [reward], and the abode of the Hereafter is better, and the abode of the Godwary is surely excellent: (30)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, those who entered Mecca and had heard the name of the Quran and the Prophet would ask various people they encountered: "ماذا انزل ربّكم" (What has your Lord revealed?). In response to these individuals, the polytheists would say: "اساطیر الاولین" (Tales of the ancients), while the believers would say: "خیراً" (Goodness/That which is good), meaning He has revealed that which is the source of goodness and felicity.

He who asks Allah for goodness (حسنة) in supplication, "ربّنا آتنا فى الدنیا حسنة" (Our Lord, grant us goodness in this world), deserves to be a person of good deeds (احسان) himself, because "للّذین احسنوا فى هذه الدنیا حسنة" (For those who do good, there is goodness in this world).

Two beautiful and clear portraits (A comparison of verses 23 to 28 and 30 to 32 of this very Surah): The Arrogant (مستكبرین) vs. The Pious (متقین)

The Arrogant (مستكبرین) The Pious (متقین)
Judgment: "اذا قیل لهم ماذا انزل ربّكم قالوا اساطیر الاولین" "و قیل للّذین اتّقوا ماذا انزل ربّكم قالوا خیراً"
Worldly Result: "فاتى اللَّه بنیانهم من القواعد" "للّذین احسنوافى هذه الدنیا حسنة"
Hereafter Punishment/Reward: "ثمّ یوم القیمة نخزیهم ... فادخلوا ابواب جهنم" "و لدار الآخرة خیر ...جنات عدن یدخلونها"
Time of Death: "تتوفاهم الملائكة ظالمى انفسهم" "الّذین تتوفاهم الملائكة طیبین"

Messages

1- Righteous judgment requires a pure and pious soul. "للّذین اتّقوا ... قالوا خیراً"

2- The Quran is introduced in a single word (goodness, virtue, the source of kindness and felicity. One word instead of a hundred). "قالوا خیراً" The call of the Quran is a call to goodness and virtue.

3- Those who do good deeds attain goodness in both worlds. "للّذین احسنوا فى هذه الدنیا حسنة ولدار الآخرة خیر"

4- The Divine reward is commensurate with human action. "للّذین احسنوا ...حسنة، للّذین اتّقوا... دار المتقین"

Ayah

جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ لَهُمْ فِيهَا مَا يَشَآءُونَ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَجْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۳۱)
the Gardens of Eden, which they will enter, with streams running in them. There they will have whatever they wish, and thus does Allah reward the Godwary (31)

Messages

1- In Paradise, gardens and rivers have variety and multiplicity. «جنات, انهار»

2- The gardens of Paradise are eternally green. They have no autumn or drought. «جنات عدن»

3- The reward for abstaining from unlawful worldly pleasures is infinite otherworldly enjoyments. «مایشاؤون»

4- «ما یشاؤون» (what they desire) is higher than «ماتشتهیه الانفس وتلذ الاعین» (what the souls desire and the eyes enjoy). This is because sometimes a person desires something that does not involve sensual or visual pleasure; rather, it is the station of gnosis (irfan) and spirituality. «لهم ما یشاؤون»

5- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to reward the virtuous. «كذالك یجزى اللَّه المتّقین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّىٰهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ طَيِّبِينَ ۙ يَقُولُونَ سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۳۲)
—those whom the angels take away while they are pure. They say [to them], ‘Peace be to you! Enter paradise because of what you used to do.’ (32)

Key Points

The meaning of al-Tayyibīn (the pure ones), considering the preceding verses which concerned the people of disbelief and polytheism: «الذین تتوفاهم الملائكة ظالمى انفسهم» (those whom the angels take away while they are wronging themselves), refers to those who were not people of polytheism and sin, and these are the same as the righteous (al-muttaqīn). Tayyib means pure from any kind of impurity or contamination, and adorned with perfections and virtues.

Messages

1- The reward and punishment for some begin from the very moment of death. «تتوفاهم الملائكة طیبین... ادخلوا الجنّة» (The angels take them away while they are pure... Enter Paradise). It is also mentioned in narrations: the grave is either a pit of Hellfire or a garden of Paradise.

2- Death is not annihilation; it is the taking of the soul. «تتوفاهم»

3- Angels are divine agents, and Allah has agents for every task. «تتوفاهم الملائكة»

4- The death of the pure ones is accompanied by the salutations of the angels. «تتوفاهم الملائكه یقولون سلام علیكم»

5- Giving salutations (salām) is the motto of the angels and a heavenly etiquette. «یقولون سلام علیكم» (In salām, there is prayer, health, and security.)

6- Paradise is given for a price (effort), not an excuse. «ادخلوا الجنّة بما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن تَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَوْ يَأْتِىَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ فَعَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۳۳)
Do they not consider that the angels may come to them, or your Lord’s edict may come? Those who were before them had acted likewise; Allah did not wrong them, but they used to wrong themselves. (33)

Key Points

A similar verse (Ayah) to this one appears in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 210, and Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 158. If the warnings of the prophets do not awaken the people, the scourges of punishment will awaken them, but what good will that do?

Messages

1- Disbelievers will not cease their denial until they witness Allah's wrath. «هل ینظرون»

2- Divine wrath in this world is for training (discipline), not for vengeance. «امر ربّك» instead of «امر اللَّه»

3- The line of polytheism (shirk), disbelief (kufr), and arrogance (istakbar) has been active throughout history. «كذلك فعل الذین من قبلهم»

4- Divine punishment is not an injustice to us; rather, our own actions were the injustice. «و ما ظلمهم اللَّه ولكن كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»

5- Disbelief and arrogance are injustices against one's own humanity. «انفسهم یظلمون»

6- What is dangerous is the continuation of oppression and transgression. «كانوا... یظلمون»

7- The descent of punishment after the completion of the proof (Hujjah) and the sending of prophets and scriptures is not merely non-oppression, but is the essence of justice itself. «و ما ظَلَمهم اللَّه»

Ayah

فَأَصَابَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۳٤)
So the evils of what they had earned visited them, and they were besieged by what they used to deride. (34)

Key Points

Sayyi'ah (سیّئه) refers to minor sins, in contrast to major sins, such as in the Ayah: «ان تجتنبوا كبائر ما تنهون نكفّر عنكم سیئات»(13). Therefore, whatever punishment reaches a person in this world is only a fraction of his deeds, and the main punishment is in the Hereafter (Qiyamah). Hāqa bihim (حاق بهم) means Halla bihim (حلّ بهم), meaning that what they used to mock befell them.

(13) An-Nisa, 31.

Messages

1- The smoke of bad deeds goes into the eyes of the person himself. «فاصابهم سیئات ما عملوا»

2- The punishment for mocking others reaches the person in this very world. «حاق بهم ماكانوا به یستهزؤن»

3- We should not mock others, lest we become afflicted ourselves. «حاق بهم ماكانوا به یستهزؤن»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ لَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَا عَبَدْنَا مِن دُونِهِۦ مِن شَىْءٍ نَّحْنُ وَلَآ ءَابَآؤُنَا وَلَا حَرَّمْنَا مِن دُونِهِۦ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ فَعَلَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ فَهَلْ عَلَى ٱلرُّسُلِ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۳٥)
The polytheists say, ‘Had Allah wished, we would not have worshiped anything besides Him—neither we, nor our fathers—nor we would have forbidden anything without His sanction.’ Those who were before them had acted likewise. Is the apostles’ duty anything but to communicate in clear terms? (35)

Key Points

The issue of determinism (Jabr) is one of the misguided justifications used by the polytheists, who claimed: Allah willed that we worship others besides Him, and if He had willed otherwise, we would not have been polytheists. This point is also raised in the words of the polytheists in Ayah 148 of Surah Al-An'am and Ayah 20 of Surah Az-Zukhruf. Question: Is the mere fact that Allah does not prevent people from deviation not a sign of His satisfaction? Answer: No, because Allah has provided the means for people's guidance; He has sent Prophets and Books. Therefore, He did not desire anyone to deviate, but neither has He compelled anyone to believe, because compulsory faith holds no value.

Messages

1- What is more dangerous than disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk) is justifying it and attributing it to Allah. «قال الّذین اشركوا لوشاء اللَّه ما عبدنا»

2- The belief in determinism (Jabr) was also present among the polytheists. «لو شاء اللَّه ما عَبَدْنا من دونه»

3- Deviants justify not only their own actions but also the actions of their ancestors and co-religionists. «نحن و لا آباؤنا»

4- Some people accept and follow the customs, traditions, and beliefs of their ancestors, whatever they may be. «نحن و لا آباؤنا»

5- Just as it is impermissible to make the forbidden lawful, it is also impermissible to forbid what Allah has made lawful. «حرمّنا من دونه من شى ء»

6- Justification is the practice of all deviants throughout history. «كذلك فعل الّذین من قبلهم»

7- Propagation (Tabligh) must be clear and explicit. «البلاغ المبین»

8- The duty of the Prophets is invitation and propagation, not compelling people to believe. «فهل على الرسل الاّ البلاغ المبین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنَا فِى كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولًا أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱجْتَنِبُوا۟ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتَ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ هَدَى ٱللَّهُ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةُ ۚ فَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۳٦)
Certainly We raised an apostle in every nation [to preach:] ‘Worship Allah, and shun fake deities.’ Among them were some whom Allah guided, and among them were some who deserved to be in error. So travel over the land and observe how was the fate of the deniers. (36)

Key Points

The word Ummah (اُمّة) derives from the root Umm (اُمّ), meaning something that joins another thing to itself. Any group that possesses a type of unity and commonality is called an Ummah. This word appears 52 times in the Quran. Taghut (طاغوت) is used to emphasize excessive transgression (tughyan). In the Quran, Taghut refers to Satan, oppressors, arrogant individuals, and idols—all sources of transgression and disobedience to Allah. This word is used both for the singular (یكفر بالطاغوت) and for the plural (اولیائهم الطاغوت). Allah has sent the means of guidance for all people: بعثنا فى كلّ اُمّة رسولاً (We raised in every community a messenger). However, a group accepts and is guided: فمنهم من هدى اللَّه (So among them were those whom Allah guided), and a group denies and goes astray: منهم من حقّت علیه الضلالة (and among them were those upon whom error was justly imposed). Thus, guidance is from Him, but deviation (or going astray) is from us. An interesting example: As the Earth revolves around the Sun, one part of it is always facing the Sun and is illuminated, while another part is turned away from the Sun and is dark. It can be said that every light the Earth possesses is from the Sun, but every darkness the Earth possesses is from itself. In any case, Allah does not attribute deviation to Himself unless the human being has prepared the ground for it, such as in the Ayah یضّل اللَّه الظالمین (Allah leads the wrongdoers astray) and the Ayah ومایضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین (And He does not lead astray thereby except the defiantly disobedient [the fasiqin]).

Messages

1- The sending of the Prophets was not restricted to a specific race, region, or tribe. بعثنا فى كلّ امّة رسولاً

2- The call to Monotheism (Tawhid) and avoidance of Taghut was at the forefront of the Prophets' mission. اعبدوا اللَّه واجتنبوا الطاغوت

3- Worshipping Allah is incompatible with accepting the dominion of the Taghuts and oppressors. اعبدوا اللَّه واجتنبوا الطاغوت

4- Through worship and connection to Allah, a person gains the power to struggle against the Taghuts. اعبدوا اللَّه واجتنبوا الطاغوت

5- Allah has given humans free will in choosing their path. فمنهم من هدى... و منهم من حقّت...

6- Travel and journeying must be a source of learning and admonition. سیروا ... فانظروا

7- History is governed by laws, and the traditions (sunan) ruling society and history are constant. سیروا... فانظروا... عاقبة المكذبین

8- The Quran has invited people to purposeful global travel. سیروا... فانظروا

Ayah

إِن تَحْرِصْ عَلَىٰ هُدَىٰهُمْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَن يُضِلُّ ۖ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۳۷)
Even if you are eager for them to be guided, indeed Allah does not guide those who mislead [others], and they will have no helpers. (37)

Key Points

Following the previous Ayah which stated that the deviation of a group is certain due to their denial and disbelief, this Ayah addresses the Prophet, saying: Do not feel sympathy for them, for your sympathy is ineffective, because Allah has sealed their hearts and they are no longer capable of guidance. Sometimes the fault lies in the people's capacity (for reception), not the preacher. The Prophet is infallible (ma'sum) and has no weakness; he possesses the best character and is also sympathetic, yet people still do not accept (the message).

Messages

1- Man sometimes reaches a point where he is neither capable of guidance in this world nor capable of help (support) in the Hereafter. «لا یهدى، من ناصرین»

2- The preacher must know that in any case, a group will not accept any logic; therefore, one should not expect the faith of all people. «ان تحرص ...لایهدى من یضلّ»

3- Intercession (Shafa'ah) on the Day of Judgment requires capacity (receptivity), not that everyone can benefit from it. «ما لهم من ناصرین»

Ayah

وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ ۙ لَا يَبْعَثُ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَمُوتُ ۚ بَلَىٰ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۸)
They swear by Allah with solemn oaths that Allah will not resurrect those who die. Yes indeed [He will], it is a promise binding upon Him, but most people do not know, (38)

Key Points

According to a narration transmitted in the book Rawdat al-Kafi from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, one of the interpretations of this Ayah is Raj'ah (the Return), in which Allah resurrects a group of people in this very world during the time of the rising of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام. However, opponents of the Shia deny it.

Messages

1- An oath is not always a sign of truth and sincerity. «اقسموا باللَّه ...لا یعلمون»

2- Worse than ignorance and disbelief is persistence and insistence upon them. «جهد ایمانهم»

3- Separating Monotheism (Tawhid) from the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the belief of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «اقسموا باللَّه ...لا یبعث اللَّه»

4- The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is the Divine appointment for reward and punishment. «وعداً علیه»

5- The resurrection of the dead on the Day of Judgment is a truth. «حقّاً»

6- The root of denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is ignorance. «لایعلمون»

Ayah

لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ كَـٰذِبِينَ (۳۹)
so that He may clarify for them what they differ about, and that the faithless may know that they were liars. (39)

Key Points

The disbelievers had doubt and disagreement regarding the Oneness of Allah, the truthfulness of the prophets, the establishment of the Resurrection, and the reckoning of deeds. This Ayah states: On the Day of Resurrection, all of this will become clear to them, and they will understand what false and incorrect beliefs they held in the world. But what good will that do?

Messages

1- The Day of Resurrection is the day when all truths become manifest. «لیبین لهم ...لیعلم الذین»

2- The foundation of disbelief (kufr) is based on falsehood (kidhb) and denial (takdhib). «الذین كفروا ...كانوا كاذبین»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا قَوْلُنَا لِشَىْءٍ إِذَآ أَرَدْنَـٰهُ أَن نَّقُولَ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (٤٠)
All that We say to a thing, when We will it, is to say to it ‘Be!’ and it is. (40)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah says, "انّما قولنا" (Indeed, Our Word), and in Ayah 82 of Surah Ya Sin, "انّما امره" (Indeed, His Command) is mentioned. And regarding the creation of Prophet 'Isa (Jesus), we read in Ayah 59 of Al 'Imran, "قال له كن فیكون" (He said to him, 'Be,' and it was). Thus, the Command (امر) and Word (قول) of Allah are the same as His Will. The word "كُن" (Be) is used to bring the concept closer to our minds; otherwise, God has no need to utter it. Just as a person, whenever he wishes to conceive of something in his mind, can create it in his mind solely through intention and needs nothing else, similarly—and Allah is far above comparison—for the creation of anything, if Allah merely wills it, it is created.

Messages

1- How can we doubt the Resurrection (Ma'ad), when everything is created by a single Will of Allah? "كن فیكون"

2- Allah brings beings from non-existence into existence, rather than creating something else by combining or changing existing things. "كن فیكون"

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا۟ فِى ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً ۖ وَلَأَجْرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (٤۱)
Those who migrate for the sake of Allah after they have been wronged, We will surely settle them in a good place in the world, and the reward of the Hereafter is surely greater, had they known (41)
ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا۟ وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (٤۲)
—those who are patient and who put their trust in their Lord. (42)

Key Points

The Quran has spoken in numerous verses about migration (Hijrah) and its effects in this world and the Hereafter. As He says in Ayah 100 of Surah An-Nisa': «من یهاجر فى سبیل اللَّه یجد فى الارض مراغماً كثیرة وسعةً» meaning, whoever migrates in the way of Allah will find many spacious places and abundance on the earth. This verse uses the expression «هاجروا فى اللَّه» (they migrated for Allah), which is naturally purer and more precise than «هجرت فى سبیل اللَّه» (migration in the way of Allah).

Messages

1- Those who abandon their homeland and wealth and migrate for the propagation of religion, the preservation of religion, or the preservation of their own lives, will achieve results. «والذین هاجروا... لنبوّئنّهم»

2- If you do not have the power to confront the oppressor, you do not have the right to accept his dominance; rather, you must migrate. «هاجروا فى اللَّه من بعد ما ظلموا»

3- Migration (Hijrah), patience (Sabr), and reliance (Tawakkul) are the keys to victory against the enemy. «هاجروا... صبروا... یتوكّلون»

4- When facing oppressors, rely on your own faith, reliance (Tawakkul), and resistance, not on foreign or external powers. «صبروا وعلى ربهم یتوكلون»

5- Ease and comfort are not achieved without enduring the hardship of migration. «هاجروا ... لنبوّئنّهم فى الدنیا حسنة»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِىٓ إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ فَسْـَٔلُوٓا۟ أَهْلَ ٱلذِّكْرِ إِن كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٤۳)
We did not send [any apostles] before you except as men to whom We revealed. Ask the People of the Reminder if you do not know. (43)

Key Points

This verse presents a general principle accepted by every human intellect: referring to people of knowledge and experts. That is, ask those who are informed and the specialists in that field about whatever you do not know. Of course, in the context of religious matters, the best manifestation of 'Ahl al-Dhikr' (People of Remembrance/Knowledge) are the Household of the Prophet (Ahl al-Bayt). Many narrations have been reported by both Shi'a and Sunni sources to this effect, which are cited in Volume 3 of Mulhaqat Ihqaq al-Haqq, starting from page 482. Tabari, Ibn Kathir, and Alusi have also identified the Ahl al-Dhikr as the Household of the Prophet in their respective commentaries under this verse. In Volume 23 of Bihar al-Anwar, starting from page 172, approximately sixty narrations are cited regarding this, in some of which the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) stated: «نحن واللَّه اهل الذكر المسئولون» (By Allah, we are the People of Remembrance who must be questioned), meaning: By God, we are the Ahl al-Dhikr whom people must ask their questions.

Messages

1- All divine Prophets were of the human species, not of the species of angels. «و ما ارسلنا ...الاّ رجالاً»

2- All Prophets were men, and specifically mature and sound men. (Although some, like Jesus and John (علیهما السلام), attained prophethood in childhood.) «رجالاً»

3- The descent of divine revelation upon a human being is not surprising; this has been Allah's tradition for the guidance of humanity. «رجالاً نوحى الیهم»

4- Ignorance is not an excuse; asking and knowing is a duty. «فسئلوا»

5- Religious matters must be asked of religious experts, not just anyone who has a slight familiarity with religion. «فسئلوا اهل الذكر»

6- Where a person already knows something, asking a question is meaningless, unless he intends to evade his own knowledge. «فسئلوا ... ان كنتم لاتعلمون»

7- If asking the Ahl al-Dhikr is necessary, then accepting their answer is also necessary; otherwise, the question is futile. «فسئلوا اهل الذكر»

8- We should ask when we ourselves are unable to know, not immediately ask about everything we don't know without any thought or reflection. The verse does not say: «فسئلوا ...ان لم تعلموا» (Ask if you do not know), but rather it says: «فسئلوا ...ان كنتم لاتعلمون» (Ask if you are unable to know).

Ayah

بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلزُّبُرِ ۗ وَأَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلذِّكْرَ لِتُبَيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ مَا نُزِّلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٤٤)
[We sent them] with manifest proofs and scriptures. We have sent down the reminder to you so that you may clarify for the people that which has been sent down to them, so that they may reflect. (44)

Key Points

"Bāyyināt" means clear proofs of prophethood and miracles, and "Zubur" is the plural of "Zabūr," meaning a heavenly book. Perhaps the Ayah refers to two types of revelation: one revelation, which is the Quran, and is for all people; and one revelation that is for the interpretation and explanation of the Quran, and is specific to the Prophet. That is, We sent down the Reminder (Dhikr) to you so that you may explain the interpretation of the Quran which was revealed to the people.

Messages

1- Prophets possessed both miracles and a book, so that people would not fall into error in distinguishing truth from falsehood. «بالبیّنات و الزُّبر»

2- Prophets were accompanied by both rational proofs ("Bāyyinah") and transmitted proofs ("Zubur"). «بالبیّنات و الزبر»

3- Although the Quran was revealed to the person of the Prophet, its audience is all people. «انزلنا الیك ...ما نزّل الیهم»

4- The Quran was revealed to the Prophet once in its entirety, and another time gradually, of which the people became aware. «انزلنا الیك...ما نزل الیهم» (In the Arabic language, "inzāl" is used for instantaneous revelation and "tanzīl" is used for gradual revelation.)

5- The Quran requires the explanation of the Prophet. Therefore, separating the Quran from the Sunnah and narrations is not permissible. «لتبیّن للنّاس»

6- The duty of the Prophet is the explanation of the Quran, and the duty of the people is to accept it based on thought and reflection. «لتبیّن للنّاس ... یتفكرون»

7- The Quran is the Dhikr (Reminder); it is a source of remembrance and attention for man, keeping him away from heedlessness and forgetfulness. «انزلنا الیك الذكر»

Ayahs

أَفَأَمِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مَكَرُوا۟ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ أَن يَخْسِفَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٤٥)
Do those who devise evil schemes feel secure that Allah will not make the earth swallow them, or the punishment will not overtake them whence they are not aware? (45)
أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ فِى تَقَلُّبِهِمْ فَمَا هُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (٤٦)
Or that He will not seize them in the midst of their bustle, whereupon they will not be able to frustrate [Him]? (46)

Key Points

|

Ayah

أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ عَلَىٰ تَخَوُّفٍ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٤۷)
Or that He will not visit them with attrition? Indeed your Lord is most kind and merciful. (47)

Key Points

Allah mentions four types of punishment in these verses for those who plot against the religion: 1- Earthly punishment: «یخسف بهم الارض» 2- Heavenly punishment: «یأتیهم العذاب» 3- Sudden punishment: «یأخذهم فى تقلبهم» 4- Spiritual punishment: «یأخذهم على تخوّف»

Messages

1- Divine punishment and retribution occur after the completion of the proof (i.e., the argument has been established). (Following the topic of prophethood in the previous verses, this verse threatens the opponents).

2- The possibility of divine punishment and retribution is sufficient reason to abandon plotting. «افأمن»

3- Those who plot against the religion should know that their cunning and deceit are futile against the knowledge and power of Allah. «افأمن الذین مكروا السیئات»

4- The time, place, and nature of Allah's wrath in this world are unpredictable, making escape impossible. «یأتیهم العذاب من حیث لایشعرون»

5- No individual or factor can prevent Allah's wrath. «فما هم بمعجزین»

6- The descent of punishment is sudden and occurs in every state and condition. «فى تقلّبهم» (Whether traveling or at home, asleep or awake, walking or standing, ...)

7- Sometimes terror, fear, and anxiety constitute divine punishment and lead to destruction. «یأخذهم على تخوّف»

8- The delay of punishment and granting respite to sinners is due to His grace, so that they may repent. «فان ربكم لرؤف رحیم»

9- Divine wrath and chastisement are also based on His nurturing and mercy. «یأخذهم... فان ربكم لرؤف رحیم»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ مَا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ مِن شَىْءٍ يَتَفَيَّؤُا۟ ظِلَـٰلُهُۥ عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَٱلشَّمَآئِلِ سُجَّدًا لِّلَّهِ وَهُمْ دَٰخِرُونَ (٤۸)
Have they not regarded that whatever thing Allah has created casts its shadow to the right and to the left, prostrating to Allah in utter humility? (48)

Key Points

The word fay' (فیى ء) refers to the shadow in the afternoon which is receding (returning), while the word dhill (ظلّ) refers to any type of shadow. The word dākhir (داخر) means submissive (or humble). Perhaps the intent of yamīn (یمین) and shimāl (شمال) in the Ayah is the two sides of the day, meaning morning and evening. Perhaps the expression of prostration (sujūd) is used for the shadow because it has a state of falling upon the ground. Although, from a cosmic (or existential) perspective, all beings are prostrate (sājid) and submissive before Allah.

Messages

1- Not only the objects themselves, but also the effects, accessories, and properties of objects, such as their shadows, are submissive before Allah. "ظلاله ... سجّداً"

2- Studying the Divine creations and their changes is one of the recommendations of the Quran. "او لم یروا الى ما خلق اللَّه ..."

3- If the objects of existence, even their shadows, are prostrate (sājid) and submissive before Allah, why should humanity not be? "سجداً ... داخرون"

4- The prostration (sujūd) and glorification (tasbīh) of existence are based on consciousness, even though we do not perceive it. "هم داخرون"

(According to Fakhr al-Rāzī, the expression "داخرون" is used for conscious beings, and this is the secret of the consciousness of beings and their shadows.)

Ayahs

وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِن دَآبَّةٍ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (٤۹)
To Allah prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, including animals and angels, and they are not arrogant. (49)
يَخَافُونَ رَبَّهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ ۩ (٥٠)
They fear their Lord above them, and do what they are commanded. (50)

Key Points

The word dābbah (دابّة) is used for a living creature that moves and transfers from one place to another. This word is applied to humans, animals, and Jinn, but it is not used for angels. The meaning of the prostration (sujūd) of all heavenly and earthly beings is either their existential submission (khuḍū‘ takwīnī) and surrender to the laws of existence, or it refers to a prostration arising from comprehension and consciousness. The apparent meaning of the verses is the second interpretation, although it is beyond our comprehension and awareness! If all beings, like the angels, are humble and prostrate before Allah, why should we humans not prostrate and instead act arrogantly?

All are bewildered and obedient for Your sake; it is not fair that you should disobey.

Messages

1- Existence is obedient and prostrate only before the One God. «للَّه یسجد»

2- Living and moving creatures also reside in the heavens. «ما فى السموات ...من دابّة» Just as we read in Ayah 29 of Surah Ash-Shura: «و ما بثّ فیهما من دابّة»

3- Angels are purely submissive to Allah. «و هم لایستكبرون»

4- Our fear of Allah is due to our sins, but the angels' fear of Allah stems from the station and grandeur of the Lord. «یخافون ربّهم من فوقهم» (Just as the Quran says regarding the believers: «واَمّا مَن خاف مقام ربّه»(14) )

5- Angels possess no spirit of arrogance, neither inherently nor in action. «لایستكبرون، یفعلون مایؤمرون»

6- The root of abandoning Divine commands is arrogance. If the root is removed, action follows. «لایستكبرون، یفعلون مایؤمرون»

7- Angels are Divine agents and are obligated (to duties). «یفعلون مایؤمرون» (14) An-Nāzi‘āt, 40.

Ayah

۞ وَقَالَ ٱللَّهُ لَا تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟ إِلَـٰهَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۖ إِنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ فَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱرْهَبُونِ (٥۱)
Allah has said, ‘Do not worship two gods. Indeed He is the One God, so be in awe of Me [alone].’ (51)

Key Points

The intent of [the command] not to take two gods is not that taking two gods is forbidden while three gods are permissible. Rather, firstly, the negation of the lesser includes the negation of the greater, and secondly, perhaps the intent of the Ayah is the belief of the polytheists who used to say: We have one God who is the Creator (Khaliq) and one God who is the Lord and Administrator (Rabb and Mudabbir), and they performed their acts of worship for the Lord God (Rabb).(15) In response to the polytheists who, a few verses earlier, used to say: If God had willed, we would not have committed polytheism, this Ayah says: Allah has forbidden you from polytheism (Shirk). So how could He have willed that you commit polytheism!?

15) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- In education (tarbiyah), one must first purify superstitious thoughts, and then speak the truth so that it may take effect. «لاتتّخذوا الهین ... انّما هو اله واحد»

2- The worship of two deities is false, let alone several deities. «لاتتخذوا الهین اثنین»

3- Dualism (Thawniyyah) and the belief in two sources of administration (tadbir) for the universe—one good and one evil (Yazdan and Ahriman)—is false. «لاتتخذوا و الهین اثنین»

4- Emphasis is necessary for negating falsehood and establishing the truth. (Although «الهین» is dual, the word «اثنین» [two] is used, and although the word «اله» [deity] is singular, the word «واحد» [one] is used.)

5- Fear of anyone other than Allah is polytheism (Shirk). «اله واحد فایّاى فارهبون»

Ayah

وَلَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَهُ ٱلدِّينُ وَاصِبًا ۚ أَفَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ تَتَّقُونَ (٥۲)
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and to Him belongs the enduring religion. Will you, then, be wary of other than Allah? (52)

Key Points

The meaning of the word "الدین" (Ad-Dīn) in the Ayah is worship and servitude, which is the necessary consequence of accepting the true religion and school of thought. And the word "واصِب" (Wāṣib) means permanent and intense, like Ayah 9 of Surah As-Saffat: «و لهم عذاب واصب»

Messages

1- Contrary to those superstitious beliefs which assumed a separate Lord (Rabb) for every type of creature, the God of everything in the heavens and the earth is one. «له ما فى السموات و الارض»

2- Worship and servitude are permissible only before the Unique Creator. «له الدین واصباً»

3- Legislation and law-making are the right of the One to Whom creation (takwīn) and origination belong. The Maker of existence must establish and articulate the law of existence. «له مافى السموات... وله الدین»

4- Polytheism (shirk) has no reason or justification. «له ما فى السموات ... له الدین... افغیر اللَّه تتقون»

Ayahs

وَمَا بِكُم مِّن نِّعْمَةٍ فَمِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِذَا مَسَّكُمُ ٱلضُّرُّ فَإِلَيْهِ تَجْـَٔرُونَ (٥۳)
Whatever blessing you have is from Allah, and when a distress befalls you, you make entreaties to Him. (53)
ثُمَّ إِذَا كَشَفَ ٱلضُّرَّ عَنكُمْ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنكُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ (٥٤)
Then, when He removes the distress from you—behold, a part of them ascribe partners to their Lord, (54)

Key Points

Ḍurr (ضُر) refers to the distress that arises from the loss of a blessing, and taj'arūn (تجئرون) is derived from ju'ār (جؤار), meaning wailing and seeking help (supplication). The content of this Ayah is also repeated in other verses of the Quran, such as in Ayah 67 of Surah Al-Isra', where He says: «و اذا مسّكم الضُّر فى البحر ضلّ من تدعون الاّ ایاه فلمّا نجاكم الى البرّ اعرضتم و كان الانسان كفوراً» (When danger strikes you in the sea, whatever you call upon vanishes, except Him. But when He delivers you safely to the land, you turn away, and indeed, man is ungrateful.) The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله says: «من لم یعلم ان للَّه علیه نعمة الاّ فى مطعم او ملبس فقد قصر عمله و دنى عذابه»(16), Whoever recognizes Allah's blessing upon him only in food or clothing has fallen short in his deeds and has drawn near to Allah's wrath.

(16) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Do not consider divine blessings to be the result of your own effort, acquisition, or management, nor chance or coincidence. «ما بكم من نعمة فمن اللَّه»

2- All blessings, even those that seem insignificant to you, are granted to you by Divine Will. «من نعمة»

3- Blessings are from Him, and in times of danger, He is the only refuge. «فمن اللَّه , الیه تجرون»

4- Calamity and hardship revive innate monotheism (worship of God). «اذا مسكم الضرّ فالیه تجرون»

5- Man is weak and becomes helpless with the slightest touch of hardship and affliction. «اذا مسّكم...»

6- Blessings are from Him, but hardships and harms are from us. «نعمة فمن اللَّه, مسّكم الضّر»

7- Faith and seeking help from Allah should not be seasonal or temporary. «اذا كشف الضُّر...فریق منكم... یُشركون»

8- Prosperity and comfort are grounds for heedlessness of the remembrance of God and inclination toward polytheism (Shirk). «كشف الضُّر ... یشركون»

9- Attention to God is universal because it is innate. «الیه تجرون», but polytheism (Shirk) is only among some. «فریق ...یشركون»

10- To attribute the kashf ḍurr (removal of harm) and the resolution of problems to any power other than Allah is Shirk (polytheism). «كشف الضُّر ... بربّهم یشركون»

Ayah

لِيَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ ۚ فَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (٥٥)
being unthankful for what We have given them. So let them enjoy. Soon they shall know! (55)

Key Points

A review of the previous verses up to this point shows the method of divine invitation and nurturing: He is the One God; «انما هو اله واحد», He is the Owner of everything; «له ما فى السموات والارض», He is the Sustainer; «له الدین», He is the Bestower of blessings; «ما بكم من نعمة فمن اللَّه», He is the Rescuer; «اذا مسكم الضُّر فالیه تجئرون». So, should we still turn to others?

Messages

1- The end result of polytheism (shirk) is disbelief in Allah and ingratitude for His blessings. «یشركون لیكفروا»

2- Turning attention to other than Allah is ingratitude for His blessings. «لیكفروا بما آتیناهم»

3- Although people are constantly ungrateful, Allah still does not withhold His blessings. «لیكفروا... فتمتعوا»

4- The end result of enjoyment mixed with disbelief is divine wrath. «فتمتعوا فسوف تعلمون»

Similar to Ayah 30 of Surah Ibrahim; «قل تمتّعوا فان مصیركم الى النار»

Ayahs

وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ نَصِيبًا مِّمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ ۗ تَٱللَّهِ لَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنتُمْ تَفْتَرُونَ (٥٦)
They assign a share in what We have provided them to what they do not know. By Allah, you will surely be questioned concerning what you used to fabricate. (56)
وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ ٱلْبَنَـٰتِ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۙ وَلَهُم مَّا يَشْتَهُونَ (٥۷)
And they attribute daughters to Allah—immaculate is He—while they will have what they desire! (57)

Key Points

This verse might refer to the content of Ayah 136 of Surah Al-An'am, where idolaters allocated a share of their agriculture and livestock to the idols: «و جعلوا للَّه مما ذَرا من الحرث و الانعام نصیباً فقالوا هذا للَّه بزعمهم و هذا لشركائنا» (And they assign to Allah a share of the crops and cattle which He created, and they say, "This is for Allah," by their claim, "and this is for our partners.") Another possibility favored by the late Allamah Tabataba'i regarding this verse is that people do not consider any role for Allah in the bestowal of blessings and the removal of calamities, seeing everything as coming from other than Him. The polytheists considered the angels to be the daughters of Allah, as He states in Ayah 19 of Surah Az-Zukhruf: «و جعلوا الملائكة الّذین هم عباد الرحمن اِناثاً» (And they made the angels, who are servants of the Most Gracious, females.) And in this belief of theirs, there were three deviations: A: They attribute offspring to Allah, while «لم یلد و لم یولد» (He begets not, nor is He begotten.) B: They imagined the angels to be daughters, while they have neither daughters nor sons. C: They, who themselves considered having a daughter a source of shame, attributed it to Allah! «أفَاَصفاكم ربّكم بالبنین و اتّخذ من الملائكة اِناثا» (Has your Lord, then, preferred you with sons and taken for Himself daughters from among the angels?) (17)

17) Al-Isra, 40.

Messages

1- Everything we possess is from Him; we must not allocate a share to other than Him, for we shall be held accountable. «و یجعلون لما لایعلمون نصیباً ... تاللَّه لتسئلنّ»

2- Allah's material grace includes even the deviated ones. «مما رزقناهم»

3- The apparent and human means upon which you rely have no knowledge themselves regarding the path of sustenance and blessing or the removal of calamity. «یجعلون لما لایعلمون نصیباً»

4- Even considering a portion of blessings to be from other than Allah is polytheism (Shirk). «نصیباً ممّا رزقناهم»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُم بِٱلْأُنثَىٰ ظَلَّ وَجْهُهُۥ مُسْوَدًّا وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ (٥۸)
When one of them is brought the news of a female [newborn], his face becomes darkened, and he chokes with suppressed agony. (58)
يَتَوَٰرَىٰ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ مِن سُوٓءِ مَا بُشِّرَ بِهِۦٓ ۚ أَيُمْسِكُهُۥ عَلَىٰ هُونٍ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُۥ فِى ٱلتُّرَابِ ۗ أَلَا سَآءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ (٥۹)
He hides from the people out of distress at the news he has been brought: shall he retain it in humiliation, or bury it in the ground! Behold! Evil is the judgement that they make. (59)

Key Points

The reasons for the hatred of ignorant Arabs towards daughters were the following matters: A: The daughter had no economic or productive role and was considered a burden on life. B: In war, upon which the fate of the tribe depended, the daughter lacked the power to fight and defend. C: In wars, daughters were taken captive and subjected to enemy violation.

Contrary to the ignorant mindset, Islam has accorded great value to the daughter, a fraction of which we mention here: The Prophet of Islam says: «نعم الولد البنات»(18) What excellent children daughters are. Allah also established the lineage of the Prophet through His daughter, Fatimah al-Zahra. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said to a man who was distressed by the birth of his daughter: Do you know better, or does God? He replied: God. The Imam said: Allah has chosen what was better for you and has given you a daughter.(19)

18) Bihar, Vol. 104, p. 94. 19) Wasa'il, Vol. 15, p. 103.

Messages

1- Humiliating and belittling the daughter is an ignorant and superstitious idea. «اذا بُشّر... بالانثى ظل وجهه»

2- Although the very birth of a child is good news (a glad tiding), superstitions consider this good news a source of humiliation. «بُشّر ... ظلّ وجهه»

3- Superstitions and false beliefs are a source of threat to families. «ظلّ وجهه... یتوارى من القوم»

4- Sometimes the greatest crimes are committed under the guise of the most sacred slogans. «أیُمسكه على هون ام یدسّه فى التراب» (He buries his child alive in the name of preserving honor.)

5- Divine religions condemn superstitious beliefs regarding daughters and women. «ساء مایحكمون»

Ayah

لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مَثَلُ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۖ وَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٦٠)
There is an evil description for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, and the loftiest description belongs to Allah, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (60)

Key Points

| Question: In Ayah 74 of this very Surah, we read: «لاتضربوا للَّه الامثال» (Do not set forth similitudes for Allah). How is it that in this Ayah He says: «و للَّه المثل الاعلى»? | Answer: The meaning of not setting forth similitudes for Allah is that you should not imagine Allah to be similar to anything. And the meaning of the highest similitude (al-Mathal al-A'la) for Allah is that the best attributes belong to Him.

Messages

1- The source of all ugliness and evil is disbelief in the Hereafter. «للذین لایؤمنون بالآخرة مثل السوء»

2- The highest perfections belong to Allah. «للَّه المثل الاعلى»

3- The beauty of man lies in faith; whenever faith departs, he becomes the germ of ugliness. «للذین لایؤمنون بالآخرة مثل السوء»

4- The might and power of Allah are accompanied by wisdom. «هو العزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّاسَ بِظُلْمِهِم مَّا تَرَكَ عَلَيْهَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ وَلَـٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (٦۱)
Were Allah to take mankind to task for their wrongdoing, He would not leave any living being upon it. But He respites them until a specified time; so when their time comes they shall not defer it by a single hour nor shall they advance it. (61)

Key Points

Although Allah's general rule is the delay of punishment, sometimes Allah chastises the oppressors so that they may serve as a lesson for others, similar to what happened to the people of Lot, Noah, and Thamud. Question: Why does the Ayah say that with the sin of man, all moving creatures (dābbah) are destroyed!? Answer: The meaning of dābbah (moving creature) in this Ayah is either exclusively human beings, similar to the Ayah: «انّ شرّ الدّواب عنداللَّه الصمّ البكم» (20), or it is because with the destruction of the human race, they (the animals) also perish, as their creation was for the sake of man.
