The infallible Imam (عليه السلام) was asked why some deceased people have their eyes open and some have them closed? He replied: Because those whose eyes were open did not find the opportunity to close them, and those whose eyes were closed did not have the opportunity to open them. «لا یستأخرون ساعة و لایستقدمون» The danger and effect of some injustices are widespread and include all moving creatures, both human and animal, just as He says: «واتّقوا فتنة لاتصیبنّ الّذین ظلموا منكم خاصة» (And fear a trial which will not afflict those who have wronged among you exclusively.) Beware of sins whose ominous effects do not afflict the oppressors alone.

20) Al-Anfal, 22.

Messages

1- Divine punishment is based on our own injustice. «یؤاخذ اللَّه ...بظلمهم»

2- Oppression is devastating and threatens the existence of the human race. «بظلمهم ما ترك علیها من دابّة»

3- Allah's tradition (Sunnah) is to grant respite and delay punishment. «یؤخرهم» In Allah's work, there is granting of time, but there is no negligence. Divine punishment may be sooner or later, but it is certain.

4- Allah's respite has a fixed term and limit. «یؤخرهم الى اجل مسمّى»

5- Allah's respite is based on His grace and is for the purpose of repentance and compensation, so do not be deceived by it. «و لو یؤاخذ ... ما ترك علیها من دابة ولكن یؤخرهم...»

6- The fact that oppressors are left free and unrestrained in their actions is not a sign of their righteousness. «یؤخرهم»

7- Man's appointed term (ajal) is unchangeable. «لا یستأخرون ساعة و لا یستقدمون»

Ayah

وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ مَا يَكْرَهُونَ وَتَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُهُمُ ٱلْكَذِبَ أَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۖ لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلنَّارَ وَأَنَّهُم مُّفْرَطُونَ (٦۲)
They attribute to Allah what they dislike [for themselves], and their tongues assert the lie that the best of rewards [in the Hereafter] will be theirs. Undoubtedly, the Fire shall be their lot and they will be foremost [in entering it]. (62)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the same superstitious belief that was raised in a few previous Ayahs: that the polytheists considered the angels to be the daughters of Allah, while they considered having a daughter a source of shame for themselves. They took pride in having a son and introduced him as a blessing better than a daughter. «و تصف السنتهم الكذب انّ لهم الحسنى»

Messages

1- We should not assign to Allah what we dislike for ourselves. «یجعلون للَّه مایكرهون»

2- Man sometimes becomes so afflicted by delusions that he imagines he is superior to others and that his ultimate fate will be better than others. «تصف السنتهم الكذب انّ لهم الحسنى» These individuals are also described in other Ayahs as such: «یحسبون انهم یحسنون صنعاً»(21) and «لئن رجعت الى ربّى انّ لى عنده للحُسنى»(22) (21) Kahf, 104. (22) Fussilat, 50.

Ayah

تَٱللَّهِ لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمَمٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ فَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٦۳)
By Allah, We have certainly sent [apostles] to nations before you. But Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them. So he is their master today and there is a painful punishment for them. (63)

Key Points

This Ayah is meant to console the Prophet of Islam, [telling him]: O Prophet, do not worry about the inattention and turning away of the people, because all the prophets have faced such individuals.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of past nations is a factor for patience, endurance, and a lesson to be learned. «ارسلنا الى امم من قبلك»

2- Allah swears by His own Name. «تاللَّه»

3- The sending of the prophets (Bi'that) has been a divine process and tradition (Sunnah). «ارسلنا الى امم من قبلك»

4- The way Satan infiltrates is by making ugliness appear attractive and beautiful, and by justifying deviations. «فزیّن لهم الشیطان اعمالهم»

5- Accepting Satan's allurements is the prelude to his domination. «زیّن ... فهو ولیّهم»

6- The guardianship (Wilayah) of Satan is not momentary; he seeks domination over people every day. «فهو ولیّهم الیوم»

7- The guardianship and domination of Satan is only in this world, and in the Hereafter, he has no power. «فهو ولیّهم الیوم»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ إِلَّا لِتُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ ۙ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٦٤)
We did not send down the Book to you except [for the purpose] that you may clarify for them what they differ about, and as a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith. (64)

Messages

1- The purpose of the revelation of the Quran is to separate truth from falsehood. «ما انزلنا ... الاّ لتبیّن لهم»

2- The source of disagreements is distance from the guidance of the prophets. «لتبیّن لهم الذى اختلفوافیه»

3- Allah completes the argument (establishes proof) through the prophets. «لتبین لهم»

4- The Quran is both a book of judgment between truth and falsehood, a means of guidance, and a means of receiving Divine mercy. «لتبین، هدى، رحمة»

5- The condition for receiving Divine guidance and mercy is that man must not have blinded the potential for felicity within himself. «لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَحْيَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَآ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ (٦٥)
Allah sends down water from the sky with which He revives the earth after its death. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who listen. (65)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the revelation of the Book, which is the source of life for hearts, was discussed. In this Ayah, the descent of rain, which causes the earth to come to life, is discussed.

Messages

1- Natural phenomena (including the arrival of clouds and rain) are not accidental; they are by the will of Allah. «واللَّه انزل»

2- The earth, like humans, experiences death and life. «فاحیا به الارض بعد موتها»

3- The execution of Divine programs occurs through ordinary and natural means. «فاحیا به الارض»

4- Water is the source of life for the earth and its inhabitants. «فاحیا به الارض»

5- Allah's management of nature is a lesson in Tawhid (Oneness of God). «انّ فى ذلك لآیة»

6- The best way to know God is to listen attentively and take heed from the Divine signs (Ayat). «لآیة لقوم یسمعون»

Ayah

وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ لَعِبْرَةً ۖ نُّسْقِيكُم مِّمَّا فِى بُطُونِهِۦ مِنۢ بَيْنِ فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ لَّبَنًا خَالِصًا سَآئِغًا لِّلشَّـٰرِبِينَ (٦٦)
There is indeed a lesson for you in the cattle: We give you a drink pleasant to those who drink, pure milk, which is in their bellies, between [intestinal] waste and blood. (66)

Key Points

Allah not only sends down life-giving water from the clouds, but He also brings forth life-giving milk from within animals. Farth (فرث) refers to the digested food inside the stomach, and ‘Ibrah (عبرة) refers to something that serves as a means of transition (crossing over) from ignorance to knowledge. Although An‘ām (انعام – livestock) is plural, the pronoun in buṭūnih (بطونه – their bellies) is singular. This is because the intent is every single unit of the livestock, not all of them collectively. Milk is a complete food that serves both as water and as sustenance, fulfilling all the body's needs. We read in narrations: Milk increases intellect, clarifies the mind, brightens the eyes, strengthens the heart, fortifies the back, and reduces forgetfulness. Livestock are not merely a means of fulfilling our material needs; rather, they can be a means for our spiritual perfection and growth in faith. Truly, can the God Who extracts milk from the heart of grass not bring forth man from the heart of the earth on the Day of Judgment?! Can the God Who extracts pure milk from between blood and digested food not separate righteous deeds from other actions? The conversion of grass into milk requires a filtration system, sterilization, removal of harmful substances, sweetening, heating, fattening, and coloring mechanisms, and plumbing within the bodies of the livestock. How is it that an oil refinery requires an engineer, but the milk refinery requires no Creator? «ان لكم فى الانعام لعبرة» (Indeed, in the livestock is a lesson for you.) Pure individuals are those who pass through ups and downs, political lines, social issues, and various friends, yet they adopt neither their color nor their scent. «من بین فرث و دمٍ لبناً خالصاً» (from between digested food and blood, pure milk). The condition for the palatability of drinks is purity. Yes, drinking water is palatable only when it is pure from all contamination. «خالصاً سائغاً للشاربین» (pure, palatable for those who drink). Palatability must be for all consumers, not just humans. Therefore, He did not say: sā’ighān lakum (palatable for you), but rather He said: sā’ighān lil-shāribīn (palatable for those who drink). In today's civilized world, it has been seen and heard that civilized countries send expired goods to refugees and the destitute! The consumption of milk by humans indicates that the One Who created the animal and fashioned the milk within it is the same One Who created us and knew our needs. «نسقیكم ممّا بطونه» (We give you to drink from what is in their bellies). Imam Ali عليه السلام wrote in Letter 25 of Nahj al-Balaghah to the official responsible for collecting Zakat: When you go to collect Zakat, you must observe certain principles, including leaving some milk in the animal's udder so that the young are not deprived. Do not separate the animal from its young, and on the journey, allow tired animals to rest and do not prevent them from eating grass and drinking water. Beating the animal, milking with long fingernails, excessive exploitation of the animal, and any form of oppression against the animal are forbidden. We read in the Quran: Prophet Solomon, despite all his grandeur, with jinn and humans under his command, showed kindness to animals and stroked their necks and legs with his blessed hand. «فطفق مسحاً بالسّوق و الاعناق»(23)

23) Sād, 33.

Messages

  1. The system of milk production in animals and its emergence from between blood and waste is a lesson for humanity. «لعبرة نسقیكم ممّا فى بطونه من بین فَرث و دم لبناً...»

Ayah

وَمِن ثَمَرَٰتِ ٱلنَّخِيلِ وَٱلْأَعْنَـٰبِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِنْهُ سَكَرًا وَرِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (٦۷)
From the fruits of date palms and vines you draw wine and goodly provision. There are indeed signs in that for people who exercise their reason. (67)

Key Points

The word sukr (سُكر) means the loss of intellect, while the word sakr (سَكر) refers to its source (the cause of intoxication). In the two preceding verses, the discussion was about water and milk, which are two natural and direct drinks. This verse speaks of drinks that we obtain by pressing fruits. Where the hand of Allah is at work, everything is grace and purity (rainwater and pure milk), but where the hand of man intervenes, sometimes good provision (rizq) is obtained, and sometimes wine, which is a source of corruption and ruin.

Messages

1- Anything that causes intoxication is not good provision (rizq). «سكراً و رزقاً حسناً»

2- What Allah has created is good; it is you who decide how to use the blessings or what actions to perform upon them. «تتخذون منه سكراً و رزقاً حسناً»

3- In the creation of fruits (the variety of their colors, tastes, and properties; their short-term and long-term benefits; their compositions; their fulfillment of human needs; the quantity of their production; the suitability of the production location for the inhabitants of the region; human access to them; and the factors involved in their emergence) there is a sign for paying attention to the Creator. «ان فى ذلك لایة»

Ayah

وَأَوْحَىٰ رَبُّكَ إِلَى ٱلنَّحْلِ أَنِ ٱتَّخِذِى مِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا وَمِنَ ٱلشَّجَرِ وَمِمَّا يَعْرِشُونَ (٦۸)
And your Lord inspired the bee [saying]: ‘Make your home in the mountains, and on the trees and the trellises that they erect. (68)

Key Points

Wahy (revelation/inspiration) means a quick and hidden sign and transfer. Besides the divine revelation to the prophets, it includes instinct, similar to this Ayah, and divine inspiration, similar to Ayah 7 of Surah Al-Qasas: «و اوحینا الى ام موسى», where Allah inspired the mother of Moses, and also satanic whispers, like Ayah 121 of Surah Al-An'am: «ان الشّیاطین لیوحون الى اولیائهم».

Messages

1- The effort and movement of animals and their choice of dwelling are based on the instinct that Allah has placed within their nature. «اوحى ربّك الى النحل ...»

2- The best type of honey is the honey of mountain bees, which utilize mountain flowers and plants. (24) (Not artificial sugary substances placed near the hive!) «اتّخذى من الجبال ... و من الشجر...» (24) Just as the best meat is fresh meat, not frozen. «لحماً طریاً» [Surah] An-Nahl, 14.

Ayah

ثُمَّ كُلِى مِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ فَٱسْلُكِى سُبُلَ رَبِّكِ ذُلُلًا ۚ يَخْرُجُ مِنۢ بُطُونِهَا شَرَابٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ فِيهِ شِفَآءٌ لِّلنَّاسِ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٦۹)
Then eat from every [kind of] fruit and follow meekly the ways of your Lord.’ There issues from its belly a juice of diverse hues, in which there is a cure for the people. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who reflect. (69)

Key Points

In this Ayah and Ayah 66, Allah has referred to two types of vital and drinkable substances that originate from within animals: one is milk and the other is honey. In Ayah 67, we read that man makes intoxicating means from fruits, «تتخذون منه سكراً» (you take from it an intoxicant), but the animal makes healing honey from the fruits!!!

In the purposeful and wise Divine system, negligence and idleness are detested. Therefore, usually wherever the issue of eating is mentioned in the Quran, a responsibility is stated alongside it, including:

«كلوا... و اعملوا صالحا»(25) Eat... and perform righteous deeds. «كلوا منها و اطعموا»(26) Eat from it and feed others. «كلوا... و اشكرواللَّه»(27) Eat... and thank Allah. «كلوا... ولا تسرفوا»(28) Eat... and do not commit extravagance.

In this Ayah too, Allah commands the honeybee to eat, but it is eating accompanied by a purpose and responsibility.

The friends (Awliyā') of Allah are like the "Nahl" (honeybee) who avoid low places and choose high ones, utilize Divine knowledge, and humbly traverse the path of their Lord. From their throats emerge wisdom and spiritual sweetness.

25) Mu’minūn, 51. 26) Hajj, 28. 27) Baqarah, 172. 28) An‘ām, 141.

Messages

1- The behavior of animals is according to the Divine command, which exists in them as instinct. «ثم كُلى»

2- Honey is the essence of various types of fruits. «كُلى من كل الثمرات»

3- Not only is the selection of habitat and food for animals guided by Allah, but all their actions follow a path that Allah has placed before them. «سُبل ربك»

4- The movement of animals along the path that Allah has chosen for them is a humble movement. «سُبل ربِك ذُللاً»

5- The miracle of creation: A honey-making factory in the belly of a small animal! «یخرج من بطونها»

6- Natural honey comes in various colors. «مختلفاً الوانه» (White, yellow, and red colors, which perhaps this difference in colors is based on the difference in the colors of the flowers.)

7- There are therapeutic properties in plants and flowers which, through honey, become a cure for our ailments. «فیه شفاء للناس» (It goes without saying that honey is not a cure for all ailments, hence it is mentioned in the indefinite form, «شفاءٌ».)

8- House-building, wax-making, and honey-making, alongside venom-making, by a small animal like the bee, are among the signs of Divine power. «لآیة»

9- All lessons and admonitions are for those who are people of thought and reflection «لقوم یتفكرون»; otherwise, there are individuals who use honey throughout their lives but are not willing to spend a few minutes thinking about it.

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يَتَوَفَّىٰكُمْ ۚ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰٓ أَرْذَلِ ٱلْعُمُرِ لِكَىْ لَا يَعْلَمَ بَعْدَ عِلْمٍ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ (۷٠)
Allah has created you, then He takes you away, and there are some among you who are relegated to the nethermost age so that he knows nothing after [having possessed] some knowledge. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-powerful. (70)

Key Points

Arthal (أرذل) is derived from rathl (رذل), meaning base or worthless. The basest period of life is that period when weakness and forgetfulness reach their peak. (29) The forgetfulness of old age relates to ordinary people, otherwise, the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah), such as Prophet Noah and Imam Mahdi (عليهما السلام), who have long lives for a specific responsibility, never suffer from heedlessness, weakness, or forgetfulness.

(29) A similar reference regarding the period of old age is repeated in Ayah 5 of Surah Al-Hajj.

Messages

1- Death and life are in His hands. «خلقكم ثمّ یتوفّاكم»

2- A useful and valuable life is the period of human awareness. A life marked by forgetfulness and heedlessness is worthless. «ارذل العمر لكى لا یعلم»

3- A long life is not for everyone. «و منكم من یرّد»

4- The changes and transformations that occur in humans—from strength to weakness, knowledge to ignorance, awareness to oblivion—are all based on the planning and knowledge of Allah. «ان اللَّه علیم قدیر»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ فَضَّلَ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فِى ٱلرِّزْقِ ۚ فَمَا ٱلَّذِينَ فُضِّلُوا۟ بِرَآدِّى رِزْقِهِمْ عَلَىٰ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ فَهُمْ فِيهِ سَوَآءٌ ۚ أَفَبِنِعْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ (۷۱)
Allah has granted some of you an advantage over others in [respect of] provision. Those who have been granted an advantage do not give over their provision to their slaves so that they become equal in its respect. What, do they deny the blessing of Allah? (71)

Key Points

Some behaviors have different effects in different situations; for example, compassion towards others is a commendable act, but sometimes, as the poet says: Compassion for the sharp-toothed leopard is tyranny against the sheep. Or, education is a commendable act, but educating some is like giving a razor to a drunken Ethiopian. However, just behavior is a value everywhere and towards everyone, and observing justice is never undesirable.

However, the meaning of justice is not that Allah places all people at the same level of talent and conditions, because if all people were equal in enjoying blessings, employment, cooperation, and collaboration—which are necessities for social life—would cease to exist. The Quran states in Ayah 32 of Surah Az-Zukhruf: «و رفعنا بعضهم فوق بعض درجات لیتّخذ بعضهم بعضاً سخریّاً» (And We have raised some of them above others in ranks, so that some may employ others in service.) meaning We gave superiority to some humans over others so that employment may arise through this means. («سُخریاً» means subjugation and employment, and «سِخریاً» means mockery.)

The difference among people in provision (Rizq) is one of the wise Divine programs, because if all people benefited equally and uniformly, their spiritual perfections would not manifest. For example, generosity, patience, sacrifice, support, compassion, humility, and the like, only gain meaning when there are differences among human beings.

It is possible that the meaning of the Ayah is that Allah is criticizing the stinginess of people, asking why the prosperous do not share their resources with their subordinates. And it is possible that the meaning of the Ayah is that it is impossible to transfer some blessings to others. For example, how can one transfer facial beauty, voice, intellect, genius, popularity, or influential speech to others? These differences are the secret to perfection and one of the Divine blessings. Why do you deny it?

Sometimes prosperity is achieved through oppression, violation of rights, and exploitation, and Islam considers such incomes unlawful (Haram). However, sometimes the expansion of provision (Rizq) is 100% God-given, through lawful means, and naturally occurring. Like two people who go fishing: hundreds of fish go into the net cast by one, while the other catches only a few fish. Here, wealth and poverty are Divine, intended for the testing of the rich and the poor and the manifestation of their talents, and they have no negative aspect. This Ayah refers to such cases. «واللَّه فضّل بعضكم على بعض فى الرزق» (And Allah has favored some of you over others in provision.)

Abu Dharr narrates from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that His Holiness said: Be equitable towards your slaves; they are your brothers. Clothe them with the clothes you wear and feed them with the food you eat. «انّما هم اخوانكم فاكسوهم مما تكسون و اطعموهم مما تطعمون»(30)

We read in narrations: Some individuals are not reformed except through wealth, and others are not reformed except through poverty and destitution. Therefore, be content with the will of the Wise Allah. «واللَّه فضّل بعضكم على بعض فى الرزق»

30) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Human provision (Rizq) is not always due to cleverness. «واللَّه فضّل بعضكم» (And Allah has favored some of you over others) just as it is stated elsewhere: «نحن قسمنا بینهم معیشتهم»(31) (It is We Who have apportioned among them their livelihood.)

2- Man is unwilling to make his subordinate a partner in his resources by dividing them. How, then, does he make stones and wood partners with Allah? «فما الّذین فضّلوا... فهم فیه سواء» (So those who were favored... they would be equal therein.)

3- Indifference and abandoning help to others is ingratitude for the blessing. «أفبنعمة اللَّه یجحدون»

31) Az-Zukhruf, 32.

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُم بَنِينَ وَحَفَدَةً وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ ۚ أَفَبِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ يُؤْمِنُونَ وَبِنِعْمَتِ ٱللَّهِ هُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ (۷۲)
Allah made for you mates from your own selves and appointed for you children and grandchildren from your mates, and We provided you with all the good things. What, do they believe in falsehood while they deny the blessing of Allah? (72)

Key Points

We read in Tafsir al-Tabari that the word «حفدة» (hafadah) refers to a son-in-law, a child, a grandchild, and even helpers, servants, and a child that the wife had from another husband. In Tafsir al-Mizan, it is stated that «حفدة» is the plural of «حافد» (hafid), meaning speed in action, and because close relatives rush faster to help their parents, they are called hafadah.

Question: Why are sons mentioned in this Ayah, but daughters are not?

Answer: It is possible that the intent of «بنون» (banūn) includes both sons and daughters. It is also possible that daughters are an instance of the tayyibāt (good things) mentioned in the Ayah, just as He says in Ayah 26 of Sūrat an-Nūr: «الطیبات للطیبین» (The good women are for the good men). Furthermore, it is possible that given the presence of the word «حفدة» (hafadah), which means a daughter's grandchild (or descendants), there was no need to specifically name daughters.

Messages

1- Spouse and children are among the blessings of Allah. (A spouse is a means of tranquility, and a child is a source of hope.) «واللَّه جعل لكم... ازواجاً ...بنین»

2- Among the favors and wisdoms of Allah is the affinity between husband and wife. «جعل لكم من انفسكم»

3- Marriage is a divinely planned program. «واللَّه جعل ...ازواجاً»

4- Allah provides both spiritual and psychological needs. «من انفسكم ازواجاً... بنین و حفدة» (from yourselves spouses... and sons and descendants), and material needs. «رزقكم من الطیبات»

5- Among the wise favors of Allah is that His provision is pleasing to the heart. «رزقكم من الطیبات»

6- Abandoning marriage, preventing childbirth, and prohibiting the lawful things of Allah constitute adherence to falsehood and ingratitude for the blessing. «افبالباطل یؤمنون و بنعمة اللَّه هم یكفرون»

7- Given the existence of a spouse and lawful provision, adultery and consuming unlawful things constitute ingratitude for the divine blessing. «بنعمة اللَّه هم یكفرون»

8- Man desires his own permanence or the permanence of his legacy. «جعل لكم من ازواجكم بنین و حفدة»

9- First, prepare your audience by stating the blessings and favors, and then criticize them. «جعل لكم من انفسكم... ازواجكم... و رزقكم ... أفبالباطل یؤمنون»

Ayahs

وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ رِزْقًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (۷۳)
They worship besides Allah what has no power to provide them with anything from the heavens and the earth, nor are they capable [of anything]. (73)
فَلَا تَضْرِبُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۷٤)
So do not draw comparisons for Allah: indeed Allah knows and you do not know. (74)

Key Points

The polytheists used to imagine Allah as a Sultan or King, and the idols as His ministers. This Ayah prohibits such analogies. Qa'ani the poet says: O You, from whom all existence has appeared, The weak dust has become powerful through You. All beings are seated beneath You, We depend on You, for You are Self-Subsistent (Qā'im bi-dhāt). Your existence is the existence of connection (to all things), not (a connection of need), You are not like anyone, nor is anyone like You.

Messages

1- The object of worship must be both the Provider (Rāziq) and the Powerful, but the partners you have taken for Allah possess neither. «یعبدون ...مالایملك»

2- Idols and objects of worship are neither providers now, nor will they be able to provide in the future. «لایستطیعون»

3- Your imaginary objects of worship possess neither the power to send down rain from the heavens nor the power to grow plants on the earth. «رزقا من السموات والارض»

4- Man-made objects of worship are not only incapable of great deeds, but they are also incapable of the smallest tasks. «رزقاً ...شیئاً»

5- The source of analogies concerning Allah is ignorance. «فلا تضربوالِلّه الامثال... انتم لاتعلمون»

Ayahs

۞ ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا عَبْدًا مَّمْلُوكًا لَّا يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ وَمَن رَّزَقْنَـٰهُ مِنَّا رِزْقًا حَسَنًا فَهُوَ يُنفِقُ مِنْهُ سِرًّا وَجَهْرًا ۖ هَلْ يَسْتَوُۥنَ ۚ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۷٥)
Allah draws a parable: a slave owned [by his master] who has no power over anything, and another one [a free man] whom We have provided a goodly provision and who spends out of it secretly and openly. Are they equal? All praise belongs to Allah. But most of them do not know. (75)
وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَآ أَبْكَمُ لَا يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ وَهُوَ كَلٌّ عَلَىٰ مَوْلَىٰهُ أَيْنَمَا يُوَجِّههُّ لَا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ ۖ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى هُوَ وَمَن يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۙ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۷٦)
Allah draws [another] parable: Two men, one of whom is dumb, having no power over anything and who is a liability to his master: wherever he directs him he does not bring any good. Is he equal to someone who enjoins justice and is [steady] on a straight path? (76)

Messages

1- Since you do not consider two humans (master and slave) equal, how can you consider the Creator and the created equal? «هل یستوى»

2- Awaken the conscience and prompt people to think by using questions and examples. «ضرب اللَّه مثلاً... هل یستون»

3- The root of people's polytheism is ignorance. «بل اكثرهم لایعلمون»

4- Freedom is a divine blessing. «لایقدر على شى ء - ینفق منه سرّاً و جهراً»

5- Good provision (rizq hasan) is beautiful when accompanied by spending (infaq). «رزقا حسنا فهو ینفق منه» And eloquent speech is valuable when accompanied by enjoining justice. «من یأمر بالعدل»

6- Secret spending is better than public spending. (The word «سرّ» [secret] precedes «جهر» [public] in the text.)

7- Speech is the key to provision. «ابكم لایقدر على شى ء، كلُّ على مولاه ... لایأت بخیر»

8- Enjoining good (or justice) is effective only when the one enjoining is also on the straight path. «یأمر بالعدل و هو على صراط مستقیم»

9- Even those who possess wealth own nothing inherently; they attained prosperity through divine provision. (In the Ayah, instead of placing the word «مالك» [owner] opposite «مملوك» [owned], the word «رزقناه» [We provided him] is used.)

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَآ أَمْرُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ إِلَّا كَلَمْحِ ٱلْبَصَرِ أَوْ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۷۷)
To Allah belongs the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. The matter of the Hour is just like the twinkling of an eye, or [even] swifter. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (77)

Key Points

The word ghayb (unseen) is contrasted with shuhūd (witnessed/present) and is a relative matter. Meaning, it is possible for something to be ghayb relative to one person, but present and manifest relative to another. Amr al-Sā'ah (the matter of the Hour), meaning the event of the Resurrection (Qiyāmah), is one of the most important instances of ghayb, and it is among Allah's favors that He has kept its time hidden from us. This verse might be a response to the analogies made by the polytheists in the previous verses: that if there were a partner for Allah in the heavens and the earth, Allah would be aware of Him, because He knows everything. After death, the human body disintegrates, and its particles scatter and become unseen (ghā'ib) in the earth. He knows the unseen (ghayb) and will create him again.

Messages

1- Allah is not only Knower of the unseen, but He is the Owner of the unseen. «للّه غیب السموات»

2- No task is difficult for Him, even the establishment of the Resurrection. «ما امر الساعة الاّ كلمح البصر»

3- The inner reality and the unseen (ghayb) of every matter will become manifest on the Day of Resurrection. «وللَّه غیب ... و ما امر الساعة»

4- The Resurrection is achievable, because Allah possesses both knowledge of everything and power over recreating. «للَّه غیب، على كلّ شى قدیر»

5- Let us not be deceived by the world; the Resurrection is not far off. Let us prepare ourselves. «كلمح البصر»

6- Allah can eliminate, compress, or accelerate the preliminaries of any matter. (32) «كلمح البصر» (32) Eliminating preliminaries, such as the speaking of Prophet Jesus in the cradle, and accelerating preliminaries, such as bringing the throne of Bilqīs (Queen of Sheba) to Prophet Solomon in an instant.

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ أَخْرَجَكُم مِّنۢ بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۙ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۷۸)
Allah has brought you forth from the bellies of your mothers while you did not know anything. He made for you hearing, eyesight, and hearts so that you may give thanks. (78)

Key Points

The word "أمهات" (ummahāt) is the plural of "أمّ" (umm). Instead of "أُمّات" (ummāt), "أمهات" (ummahāt) is used, meaning the letter 'hā' (ه) is extra in the plural form.

Messages

1- Birth from the mother is by Divine Will. «اللَّه اخرجكم من بطون امهاتكم»

2- Remembering past deficiencies, shortcomings, and lack of knowledge cultivates the spirit of gratitude (shukr) in man. «لاتعلمون شیئاً... لعلكم تشكرون»

3- The wisdom and tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is that every task is accomplished through its means. The tools of knowledge are the eye, the ear, and the heart. «جعل لكم السّمع...»

4- The functionality of the ear precedes that of the eye. (The ear hears even in the mother's womb, but the eye remains closed for a period after birth. Perhaps this is why the ear is mentioned before the eye in the Ayah.) «جعل لكم السمع والابصار»

5- Gratitude for any blessing is its proper utilization. This is because the Quran criticizes a group who have eyes but do not see the truths, and have ears but are unwilling to hear the truth. «جعل لكم السمع والابصار ... لعلكم تشكرون»

6- The true gratitude for the blessing of the eye and the ear is the acquisition of knowledge. This is because the Ayah first states: you knew nothing, and I gave you eyes and ears so that you may be grateful, meaning, acquire knowledge. «جعل لكم السمع و الابصار... لعلكم تشكرون»

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلطَّيْرِ مُسَخَّرَٰتٍ فِى جَوِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَا يُمْسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۷۹)
Have they not regarded the birds disposed in the air of the sky: no one sustains them except Allah. There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (79)

Key Points

As mentioned, the portrait of this Surah is the recognition of blessings and attention to their Creator. Up to this point, blessings such as rainwater, animal milk, fruits, honey, and the blessing of spouse and children have been mentioned. In this Ayah and the next Ayah, reference is made to the blessing of birds, the hides, wool, and fur of livestock, and the utilization of mountains. The flight of birds is sometimes solitary and sometimes collective, sometimes organized and sometimes disorganized, sometimes for escape and sometimes for food. Every bird flies with wings appropriate to its weight and need, and all these matters should cause the rational human being to turn His attention toward Allah.

Messages

1- Allah demands that we reflect upon creation and criticizes those who do not think. «الم یروا»

2- Birds, in every state and everywhere, are subjugated by the Divine. «مسخرات فى جو السماء»

3- Possessing the means and tools for flying in the sky should not make us heedless of God.(33) «ما یمسكهنّ الاّ اللَّه»

4- The wonders of bird flight are not just one or two. «لآیات» However, the realization of these signs is for the people of faith. «لقوم یؤمنون»

33) One day I was sitting on an airplane. The chief flight attendant announced that we would be landing at the airport in a few minutes. I asked him why he did not say Insha'Allah (if God wills)? He replied: The airplane's computer has clarified the time, distance, altitude, and everything else for us, and there is no need for Insha'Allah. I told him: Do not the airplanes that crash in the East and West of the world have computers? Yes, the Will of God does not wait for your computer. He thought for a moment, returned, and announced that in a few moments, Insha'Allah, we would land. I thanked him with a gift.

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنۢ بُيُوتِكُمْ سَكَنًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّن جُلُودِ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ ۙ وَمِنْ أَصْوَافِهَا وَأَوْبَارِهَا وَأَشْعَارِهَآ أَثَـٰثًا وَمَتَـٰعًا إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۸٠)
It is Allah who has made your homes as a place of rest for you and He made for you homes out of the skins of the cattle which you find portable on the day of your shifting and on the day of your halt, and out of their wool, fur and hair [He has appointed] furniture and wares [enduring] for a while. (80)

Key Points

The word "Bayt" (بیت) is derived from "Baytūtah" (بیتوته), meaning a place of rest where a person spends the night. The word "Ẓa'n" (ظعن) means departure (or going). The word "Athāth" (اثاث) means the abundant goods/furnishings of the house. The intent of "tastakhiffūnahā" (تستخفونها) refers to tents whose transport is easy and light.

Messages

1- The home is a place of comfort and tranquility. "buyūtakum sakanan" (بیوتكم سكناً)

2- We should not overlook the role of livestock, their hides, and their down, hair, and wool in preparing the necessities of life and tents. "matā‘an" (متاعاً)

3- The time for utilizing blessings is limited. "ilā ḥīn" (الى حین)

4- The harmony between blessings and human needs, and the suitability of the human structure with hides, wool, down, and hair, is one of the Divine provisions. "ja‘ala lakum - ja‘ala lakum" (جَعَل لكم - جَعَل لكم)

5- The light weight of wool, hides, down, and hair is one of Allah's blessings. "tastakhiffūnahā" (تستخفونّها)

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّمَّا خَلَقَ ظِلَـٰلًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ أَكْنَـٰنًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ سَرَٰبِيلَ تَقِيكُمُ ٱلْحَرَّ وَسَرَٰبِيلَ تَقِيكُم بَأْسَكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْلِمُونَ (۸۱)
It is Allah who made for you the shade from what He has created, and made for you retreats in the mountains, and made for you garments that protect you from heat, and garments that protect you from your [mutual] violence. That is how He completes His blessing upon you so that you may submit [to Him]. (81)

Key Points

The word 'aknān' (أكنان) is the plural of 'kunn' (كُنّ), which refers to anything used for concealment (shelter). What is meant by it in this Ayah are the caves and tunnels beneath the mountains. In the previous Ayah, Allah mentioned the blessing of dwelling for city dwellers: «جعل لكم من بیوتكم سكناً» (He made for you, from your houses, a place of rest), and the blessing of tents for nomads: «جعل لكم من جلود الانعام بیوتا» (He made for you, from the hides of cattle, houses). In this Ayah, He mentions the blessing of caves for cave dwellers: «جعل لكم من الجبال اكناناً» (He made for you, from the mountains, shelters). Among the types of clothing, the name 'sarābil' (سرابیل), meaning shirt or garment, is mentioned. This is permanent clothing for men and women, children and adults, at all times and in all places, covering the entire body. According to Tafsir al-Mizan, it is customary for Arabs to mention one of two opposing things while intending both. Therefore, in this Ayah, the phrase «تقیكم الحر» (protects you from the heat) refers to clothing that protects a person from both cold and heat, even though only heat is specifically named.

Messages

1- Paying attention to and remembering blessings increases a person's affection for the Bestower of blessings and revives the spirit of submission and worship within him. (The entire Ayah)

2- The role of shade is no less important than that of light. «جعل لكم... ظلالاً»

3- Enjoying blessings should not make us heedless of remembering their Creator. (The word 'ja'ala' (جَعَل) is used three times and the word 'khalaqa' (خَلَق) is used once in this Ayah.)

4- Armor and defensive clothing, made through the softening of iron and ingenuity, are among the Divine favors. «جعل لكم سرابیل تقیكم بأسكم»

5- Drawing people's attention to the blessings and their Creator is the best way to invite them to Allah and prevent rebellion and arrogance. «یُتّم نعمته علیكم لعلكم تسلمون»

6- In discussions, consider the audience and the context of the speech. (Although clothing is also a means of protection from cold and a means of beauty, because the primary issues in the Hijaz region were heat and warfare, Allah referred to the two benefits of protection from heat and protection in war.) «سرابیل تقیكم الحرّ... تقیكم بأسكم»

Ayah

فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۸۲)
But if they turn their backs [on you], your duty is only to communicate in clear terms. (82)

Messages

1- Man has free will in choosing his path and belief, and the sign of this is rebellion against the invitation of the prophets. «فان تولّوا»

2- The duty of the prophets is conveyance (of the message), not compulsion. «فانّما علیك البلاغ»

3- Propagation must be clear and without ambiguity or doubt. «البلاغ المبین»

4- If man does not possess a sound soul, the clearest propagations, «البلاغ المبین», coming from the purest individuals—namely the prophets—will not be effective in him. «فان تولّوا»

Ayah

يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُنكِرُونَهَا وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸۳)
They recognize the blessing of Allah and then deny it, and most of them are faithless. (83)

Key Points

The Quran has spoken many times about the knowing denial and obstinacy against the truth; Sometimes He says: even though they are inwardly certain, they deny it, «و جحدوا بها واستیقنتها انفسهم»(34) Sometimes He says: they recognized the Prophet just as they recognized their own sons, but they denied Him, «یعرفونه كما یعرفون ابنائهم»(35) Sometimes He says: they know the truth, but they knowingly conceal it, «لیكتمون الحقّ و هم یعلمون»(36) And sometimes He says: they understand the matter but they cover it up, «فلما جائهم ما عرفوا كفروا به»(37) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: When Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام gave his ring to a poor person in the Prophet's mosque while in Ruku' (bowing), and Ayah 55 of Surah Al-Ma'idah was revealed, stating that your Wali (Guardian) is only Allah, His Messenger, and the one who gives charity while bowing, a group recognized their Wali but denied him. At this point, this Ayah was revealed: «یعرفون نعمت اللَّه ثمّ ینكرونها».(38) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «نحن واللَّه نعمت اللَّه التى انعم بها على عباده و بنا یفوز من فاز»(39) By God, we are the blessing (Ni'mah) that Allah has bestowed upon His servants, and only through us will those who succeed achieve salvation.

34) Naml, 14. 35) Baqarah, 146. 36) Baqarah, 146. 37) Baqarah, 89. 38) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 39) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 245.

Messages

1- Knowledge and recognition are not enough; acceptance and action are necessary. «یعرفون .. ینكرونها»

2- The root of disbelief (kufr), besides ignorance, includes tribal prejudices, spiritual obstinacy, and the preservation of material interests. «یعرفون ... ینكرون، ... الكافرون»

3- After recognizing the truth, there is a period of conflict and struggle within the soul until wretchedness finally prevails and they commit disbelief. «ثمّ ینكرونها» (then they deny it) and He did not say: «فینكرونها» (so they deny it).

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا ثُمَّ لَا يُؤْذَنُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ (۸٤)
The day We shall raise a witness from every nation, the faithless will not be permitted [to speak], nor will they be asked to propitiate [Allah]. (84)

Key Points

In the Quran, the topic of the witnesses of the Day of Judgment has been raised repeatedly; the Prophets, the angels, the friends of Allah (Awliya), the earth, and the body parts are among the witnesses of that Day. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: For every nation and every time, there is an Imam with whom people will be resurrected (40). And Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: «نحن الشهود على هذه الامة» (Indeed, We are the witnesses over this nation). Although Allah is present everywhere and aware of everything, the existence of multiple witnesses is a factor in generating piety (taqwa) and modesty (haya) for the believers, and a means for greater disgrace for the criminals. The condition for testimony and witnessing is knowledge and awareness accompanied by justice. Therefore, the friends of Allah (Awliya) who will be witnesses over us on the Day of Judgment must have observed our deeds in this world, and our actions must be presented to them. This is our belief, which, according to numerous narrations and the blessed Ayah «فسیرى اللَّه عملكم و رسوله و المؤمنون» (41), our deeds are presented to Imam Mahdi عليه السلام every week. Without this belief, the verses regarding "witnessing" and testimony on the Day of Judgment are not justifiable. How can someone who is unaware of our deeds or lacks justice give testimony on the Day of Judgment?

Istī‘tāb (استعتاب) is derived from ‘itāb (censure); it means the sinner requests censure and reproach from the rightful authority so that His wrath subsides and He grants satisfaction.

In this world, one can repent, apologize, and make amends, but on the Day of Judgment, there is no room for justification, apology, or making amends:

As for justification, it is rejected, because when some inhabitants of Hell say to another group: «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین» (42) (Had it not been for you, we would have been believers), the response comes: «بل لم تكونوا مؤمنین» (43) (Nay, but you yourselves were not believers).

As for making amends for sin, it is rejected, because when they ask Allah: «فارجعنا نعمل صالحاً» (44) (Send us back so we may do righteous deeds), they receive the answer: «كلا انها كلمة هو قائلها» (45), «فذوقوا بما نسیتم» (46).

And as for apology, it is rejected, because the Quran states: «ولایؤذن لهم فیعتذرون» (47) (Nor will they be permitted to offer excuses).

Messages

1- The witness for every nation is from among themselves, so that the proof (Hujjah) is established against them completely. «من كلّ امّة شهیداً»

2- Allah has a proof and a witness over the people in every nation and in every time. «من كلّ امّة شهیداً»

3- In the Divine Court, accountability, reckoning, and witnessing (shahadah) are relevant. «شهیداً»

4- With the existence of a witness and a proof (Hujjah), there is no longer any meaning for making excuses, justification, or seeking satisfaction (Istī‘tāb). «لایؤذن ... لا هم یستعتبون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَءَا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ فَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ (۸٥)
And when the wrongdoers sight the punishment, it shall not be lightened for them, nor will they be granted any respite. (85)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that on the Day of Resurrection, neither will permission for justification be given, nor will the possibility of seeking satisfaction (or reconciliation) exist. And in this Ayah, He says: the wrath of Allah on that Day will have neither reduction nor delay.

Messages

  1. The source of Allah's punishment is the oppression (injustice) committed by humanity itself. «رأ الذین ظلموا العذاب»

  2. Awareness of the punishment and its nature is a deterrent factor against sin. «اذا رأ الذین ظلموا...»

  3. On the Day of Resurrection, the punishment of the oppressors will have neither reduction nor delay. «الذین ظلموا...فلا یخفّف عنهم و...»

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَءَا ٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ شُرَكَآءَهُمْ قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ شُرَكَآؤُنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كُنَّا نَدْعُوا۟ مِن دُونِكَ ۖ فَأَلْقَوْا۟ إِلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَوْلَ إِنَّكُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۸٦)
When the polytheists sight their partners, they will say, ‘Our Lord! These are our partners whom we used to invoke besides You.’ But they will retort to them, ‘You are indeed liars!’ (86)

Key Points

According to narrations, there are various stations (Mawāqif) on the Day of Judgment. In some stations, mouths are sealed, and hands and feet bear witness. In another station, wails and pleas for help are loud. In yet another station, man seeks to place his sins upon others. For example, he says to Satan: You compelled me toward disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk). Satan replies: I was opposed to your polytheism from the very beginning. «انى كفرت بما اشركتمون من قبل»(48) Sometimes, man considers the idols to be the culprits, but they too disavow themselves. «و یوم القیامة یكفرون بشرككم»(49) Even Allah addresses Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام): Did you tell the people to take you and your mother as deities? Jesus replies: You are exalted above every partner... These people went after the jinn, and I never invited anyone toward other than You. «قال سبحانك ما یكون لى اِن أقول ما لیس لى بحق»(50) And in the current verse under discussion, the beings that were taken as partners of Allah disavow the polytheists and deny their claims.

48) Ibrahim, 22. 49) Fatir, 14. 50) Ma'idah, 116.

Messages

1- Allah has no partner; the polytheists merely imagine partners for Him. (This is indicated by the use of) «شركائهم» (their partners) and not «شركاء اللَّه» (partners of Allah).

2- The terror of the Day of Judgment causes man to shift the burden onto others in order to save himself. «هؤلاء شركاؤنا» (These are our partners)

3- Fabricated deities will also be resurrected on the Day of Judgment. «هؤلاء شركاؤنا الّذین كنّا ندعوا» (These are our partners whom we used to invoke)

4- On the Day of Judgment, even the idols will speak in defense of themselves. «فالقوا الیهم القول...» (Then they cast the word back to them...)

5- The idols deny their worthiness of worship and tell the idolaters: You were, in reality, worshipping your own imaginations. «فالقوا الیهم القول انكم لكاذبون» (Then they cast the word back to them: Indeed, you are liars)

Ayah

وَأَلْقَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلسَّلَمَ ۖ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۸۷)
They will submit to Allah on that day, and what they used to fabricate will forsake them. (87)

Messages

1- If we do not submit today, tomorrow on the Day of Resurrection we will submit, but what good will that do? «والقوا الى اللَّه یومئذ السّلم»

2- All wishful thinking will vanish on the Day of Resurrection. (The hope for intercession, proximity, and the expectation of honor and help from other than Allah, is nothing more than a fantasy.) «ضلّ عنهم ما كانوا یفترون»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ زِدْنَـٰهُمْ عَذَابًا فَوْقَ ٱلْعَذَابِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يُفْسِدُونَ (۸۸)
Those who are faithless and bar from the way of Allah—We shall add punishment to their punishment because of the corruption they used to cause. (88)

Key Points

People's reactions to the path of Allah are varied, sometimes positive and sometimes negative, and each has degrees and stages.

A: Positive Reactions: - A group awaits understanding the path of Allah and traversing it. «عسى ربّى ان یهدینى سواءالسبیل»(51) - A group migrates for the sake of Allah. «و من یهاجر فى سبیل اللَّه»(52) - A group is besieged because they are in the path of Allah. «احصروا فى سبیل اللَّه»(53) - A group endures persecution in the path of Allah. «اوذوا فى سبیلى»(54) - A group becomes callers inviting others to the path of Allah. «ادع الى سبیل ربّك»(55) - A group never weakens in the path of Allah. «فما وهنوا لما اصابهم فى سبیل اللَّه»(56) - A group fights in the path of Allah. «یقاتلون فى سبیل اللَّه»(57) - A group is killed in the path of Allah. «قتلوا فى سبیل اللَّه»(58)

B: Negative Reactions: - A group imagines they are on the path of Allah. «یحسبون انهم مهتدون»(59) - A group desires the path of Allah to be crooked. «یبغونها عوجا»(60) - A group blocks the path of Allah for others. «یصدّون عن سبیل اللَّه»(61) - A group spends wealth to block the path of Allah. «ینفقون اموالهم لیصدوا عن سبیل اللَّه»(62)

Messages

1- Disbelief (Kufr) is the prelude to corruption (Fasad). «كفروا و صدوا ... یفسدون» Just as faith (Iman) is the prelude to righteous deeds. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» (which is mentioned in numerous verses).

2- The reckoning of the leaders of disbelief, who block the path of Allah using the pen, speech, and resources, is separate from the reckoning of ordinary individuals. «زدناهم عذاباً فوق العذاب»

3- Corrupters on Earth (Mufsid fil-Ard) are not only those who disrupt societal security, but rather any individual or group that, in any form, prevents the spiritual growth of society, is a corrupter. «الّذین كفروا و صدّوا ... بما كانوا یفسدون»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ فِى كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا عَلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ شَهِيدًا عَلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ تِبْيَـٰنًا لِّكُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۸۹)
The day We raise in every nation a witness against them from among themselves, We shall bring you as a witness against these. We have sent down the Book to you as a clarification of all things and as guidance, mercy and good news for the Muslims. (89)

Key Points

The issue of the witness over the nations was also mentioned five verses prior, and this is part of our definite beliefs that on the Day of Judgment, every nation will have a witness, and the Prophet of Islam is the witness over this nation and the witnesses of the other nations. The important point is that the testimony of the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) must be based on perception, sight, and knowledge, and they must be infallible (ma'soom) from every error and falsehood, so that their testimony on that sensitive day serves as a conclusive argument (Itmam al-Hujjah) against all people. And it is obvious that no one other than the infallible Prophets and Imams, who are aware of the behavior, deeds, words, and intentions of the people through divine assistance (ghaybi aids), can be aware of the deeds of the present and the absent, the apparent and the hidden among the people, and whose word will be accepted by the nation and by Allah on the Day of Judgment.

In Tafsir Safi, it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that he said: By Allah, we know everything in the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, and whatever is in Paradise and Hell. Then the Imam recited this verse three times consecutively.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Everything the nation needs has come in the Quran, and if you ask for the Quranic reference for any statement you hear from me, I will provide it. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Your news, the history of those who passed away, those who are to come, and the heavens and the earth are all contained in the Quran.(63)

Imam Reza عليه السلام, in a session with the heads of religions, said: Among the miracles of the Prophet of Islam is that a poor orphan and an uneducated shepherd should possess a Book that is a "Tibyān Kulli Shay'" (an explanation of everything), and in which the news of the past and the future until the Day of Judgment is recorded.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: For every issue over which two people disagree, there is a principle in the Book of Allah that resolves it, even if people's intellects cannot grasp it.(64)

The Quran is the explanation of everything, but not everyone understands it, just as Imam Ali عليه السلام says: In the Quran, there are apparent expressions for the common people, symbolic allusions for the elite, divine subtleties (Latā’if Ilāhī) for the friends of Allah, and realities (Haqā’iq) for the Prophets.(65)

The comprehensiveness (Tibyān) of everything is either direct or through verses in which principles are presented, and those principles are effective for us. Such as the verse: «ما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه و ما نهاكم عنه فانتهوا» (66) (Take what the Messenger gives you, and refrain from what He forbids you), and such as the verse: «لتبین للناس ما نزّل الیهم» (67)
