Messages

  1. The constant supervision of the friends of Allah over our deeds is one of the certainties of the Quran. «شهيداً عليهم»
  2. Witnesses on the Day of Judgment have a hierarchy. The Imam of every nation is its witness, and the Prophet of Islam is the witness over all witnesses. «جئنا بك على هؤلاء شهيداً»
  3. The Prophet of Islam is both a witness over all Prophets, and His Book is the explanation of everything (Tibyān Kulli Shay'). «نزلنا عليك الكتاب تبيانا لكل شيئ»
  4. The Quran is a comprehensive Book for explaining all the needs of society. «تبياناً لكلّ شيئ»
  5. Submission (to Allah) is the prerequisite for receiving divine guidance, mercy, and glad tidings. «هدى و رحمة و بُشرى للمسلمين»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْعَدْلِ وَٱلْإِحْسَـٰنِ وَإِيتَآئِ ذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَيَنْهَىٰ عَنِ ٱلْفَحْشَآءِ وَٱلْمُنكَرِ وَٱلْبَغْىِ ۚ يَعِظُكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (۹٠)
Indeed Allah enjoins justice and kindness, and generosity towards relatives, and He forbids indecency, wrongdoing, and aggression. He advises you, so that you may take admonition. (90)

Key Points

This Ayah is the universal charter of Islam, which Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) used to recite in the Friday prayer sermons. According to Fayd Kashani in Tafsir al-Safi; if the Qur'an contained only this one Ayah, it would be sufficient for us to say: the Qur'an is "a clarification of all things" (تبيان كلّ شىء). The commands and prohibitions of this Ayah have existed in all religions and have never been abrogated. Walid ibn Mughirah was so captivated by this Ayah that he said: Its sweetness, beauty, and content are such that it cannot be the speech of a human being.(68) And Uthman ibn Maz'un says that upon hearing this Ayah, Islam penetrated the depths of his soul and settled in his heart.

'Adl (عدل) refers to something whose equality is perceived through insight, while 'Idl (عِدل) refers to something whose equality is perceived through the senses. 'Adl is equality in recompense, but 'Ihsan is increasing the reward.(69) 'Adl is the avoidance of extremism and negligence, which applies both to beliefs and to personal and societal conduct. The recommendation in this Ayah is directed both at the individual and at governments. 'Adl in the system of creation is the secret of its stability. "By justice (al-'Adl) the heavens and the earth stand." And in the system of legislation (Shari'ah), it is the cornerstone of the call of all the Prophets. 'Ihsan is a blessed word that includes financial, intellectual, cultural, and emotional services. And 'Fahsha' (فحشاء) refers to a sin that is great and scandalous, and 'Munkar' (منكر) refers to an act that reason, innate nature (fitrah), and the Divine Law (Shari'ah) reject. According to Tafsir Fi Zilal, whenever tyrants (taghuts) use corrupt propaganda to diminish the ugliness of sin in the eyes of the people and reduce public sensitivity toward sin, the criterion for Ma'ruf (good) and Munkar (evil) will be the Divine Law (Shari'ah).

68) Tafsir Kabir. 69) Mufradat Raghib.

Messages

1- Is it possible that the God Who invites humanity to justice ('Adl) and goodness ('Ihsan') is Himself not just? «ان اللَّه یأمر بالعدل و الاحسان»

2- Justice and goodness together possess attraction; otherwise, dry regulations do not heal hearts. «یأمر بالعدل و الاحسان» ('Adl is the obligatory amount, and 'Ihsan' is the recommended amount.)

3- The first One Who commands good and forbids evil is Allah Himself. «انّ اللَّه یأمر ... و ینهى»

4- In goodness ('Ihsan'), attending to relatives and maintaining kinship ties (Silat al-Rahim) has priority. «الاحسان و ایتاء ذى القربى»

5- Commanding good precedes forbidding evil, and justice precedes goodness. «یأمر بالعدل و الاحسان ... و ینهى»

6- Commanding good and forbidding evil must take place in an atmosphere of sincerity and affection, and in the form of admonition and goodwill. «یأمر ... ینهى ...یعظكم»

7- Those who command good should not expect one hundred percent acceptance from the people. «لعلّكم تذكّرون»

8- People are innately inclined toward justice and goodness and are repulsed by immorality (Fahsha') and evil (Munkar), but temptations and instincts cause their heedlessness, which must be remedied by reminding them. «لعلّكم تذكّرون»

9- In forbidding evil, one must first prevent scandalous sins, just as in commanding good, one must first recommend the obligatory duties. «یأمر بالعدل ... ینهى عن الفحشاء»

10- The recommendation of justice and the prohibition of immorality are the eternal tradition of Allah. «یأمر ... ینهى ... یعظكم» (The present tense verb indicates continuity.)

Ayah

وَأَوْفُوا۟ بِعَهْدِ ٱللَّهِ إِذَا عَـٰهَدتُّمْ وَلَا تَنقُضُوا۟ ٱلْأَيْمَـٰنَ بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِهَا وَقَدْ جَعَلْتُمُ ٱللَّهَ عَلَيْكُمْ كَفِيلًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۹۱)
Fulfill Allah’s covenant when you pledge, and do not break [your] oaths, after pledging them solemnly and having made Allah a witness over yourselves. Indeed Allah knows what you do. (91)

Key Points

In narrations, the covenant mentioned in the Ayah is understood to be the covenant between the people and the Divine leaders. (70) If people are faithful to their covenants, Allah will put into action the favors He has promised. «أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ» (71)

70) Tafasir Safi and Durr al-Manthur. 71) Baqarah, 40.

Messages

1- Fidelity to Divine covenants and oaths is necessary. «أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ ...»

2- Fulfilling an oath or covenant is required only when there is serious intent involved. «بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِهَا» As we read in Ayah 89 of Surah Al-Ma'idah: Allah does not hold you accountable for frivolous oaths. «لَا يُؤَاخِذُكُمُ اللَّهُ بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمَانِكُمْ»

3- Allah knows your breach of covenant and will punish it, whether people realize it or not. «إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ»

Ayah

وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّتِى نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَهَا مِنۢ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ أَنكَـٰثًا تَتَّخِذُونَ أَيْمَـٰنَكُمْ دَخَلًۢا بَيْنَكُمْ أَن تَكُونَ أُمَّةٌ هِىَ أَرْبَىٰ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ ۚ إِنَّمَا يَبْلُوكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦ ۚ وَلَيُبَيِّنَنَّ لَكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (۹۲)
Do not be like her who would undo her yarn, breaking it up after [spinning it to] strength, by making your oaths a means of [mutual] deceit among yourselves, so that one community may become more affluent than another community. Indeed Allah tests you thereby, and He will surely clarify for you on the Day of Resurrection what you used to differ about. (92)

Key Points

It is narrated in history that a foolish woman would weave cotton and wool during half the day, and during the other half, she would unravel what she had woven. Her action became a proverb, which the Quran also uses for those who waste their efforts and break their covenants. Fatimah Zahra علیها السلام recited this verse in a sermon she delivered against the rulers of the time in the Mosque of Medina, stating that your example, due to your covenant-breaking regarding the allegiance you gave to Ali علیه السلام at Ghadir Khumm, is like the example of that woman who would weave and then unravel what she had woven. (72) The parables and proverbs of the Quran are such that they maintain their freshness, clarity, and naturalness in all places and times, satisfying everyone from the elementary school child to the philosopher. The phrase «أن تكون اُمّة هى اربى من اُمّةٍ» can be interpreted in two ways: A: Do not break a covenant you have made with a smaller group merely because of the power and multitude of your own group. B: Do not break your covenant with smaller groups merely because you have become a superpower.

72) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Covenant-breaking is the wasting of past efforts. «نقضت غزلها»

2- The stronger the covenant is made, the uglier its breaking is. «من بعد قوّةٍ»

3- Repeated covenant-breaking carries greater disgrace. («انكاثاً» is the plural of «نكث»)

4- Let us not play with sacred things. (Let us not use sacred things as a pretext or a means of deception) «تتخذون ایمانكم دخلاً»

5- Power, numbers, and political and economic clamor are grounds for covenant-breaking. «ان تكون اُمة هى اربى من اُمّةٍ»

6- One of the tools of trial is fulfilling commitments. «یبلوكم اللَّه به»

7- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor in piety (Taqwa) and observing covenants. «و لیبیّننّ لكم یوم القیامة»

Ayah

وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً وَلَـٰكِن يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَلَتُسْـَٔلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۹۳)
Had Allah wished, He would have made you one community, but He leads astray whomever He wishes and guides whomever He wishes, and you will surely be questioned concerning what you used to do. (93)

Key Points

Allah guides all people naturally (by innate disposition). However, based on the choice and freedom they possess, people divide into two groups. One group chooses the path of transgression, contrary to reason and innate disposition, and does not repent. Allah then abandons them and misguides them. But the other group chooses the path of innate disposition and obedience, and Allah helps and guides them. Therefore, the meaning of Allah's guidance and misguidance is the stage that follows human choice. For example, the teacher's goal on the first day of teaching is to educate all students. However, the teacher gives extra private time to the group that strives to understand, but leaves the playful student to his own devices. Truly, if human guidance and misguidance were not based on choice, Allah would not have said at the end of the Ayah: You will be questioned! Thus, Allah's questioning is a sign of our freedom, because a compelled person is not held accountable.

Messages

1- Allah has the power to compel people, but His tradition and plan is to leave people free. «و لو شاء اللَّه لجعلكم»

2- Man is free in choosing his belief and path. «لتسئلن»

3- We must take the questioning and answering on the Day of Judgment seriously. «و لتسئلنّ»

4- The questioning and accountability cover all human actions. «عمّا كنتم»

Ayah

وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَكُمْ دَخَلًۢا بَيْنَكُمْ فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌۢ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا وَتَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلسُّوٓءَ بِمَا صَدَدتُّمْ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَكُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۹٤)
Do not make your oaths a means of [mutual] deceit among yourselves, lest feet should stumble after being steady and [lest] you suffer ill for barring from the way of Allah and face a great punishment. (94)

Key Points

The word "صددتّم" (ṣadadtum), according to Raghib, means both being held back and holding others back. And the word "دَخَلَ" (dakhala) has the same weight and meaning as "دَغَل" (daghal).

Messages

1- Whenever a danger is widespread and serious, repeat your recommendations and prohibitions. (In Ayah 91, the misuse of oaths was also prohibited.) «لاتتخذوا ایمانكم دخلاً»

2- Covenant breaking and the misuse of sacred things lead to a bad end. «فتزلّ قدم بعد ثبوتها»

3- One sin becomes the ground and context for other sins. (Covenant breaking is the prelude to a bad end, and that, in turn, is the prelude to being held back and holding others back from the path of Allah.) «صددتم عن سبیل اللَّه»

Ayah

وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا۟ بِعَهْدِ ٱللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۚ إِنَّمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۹٥)
Do not sell Allah’s covenants for a paltry gain. Indeed, what is with Allah is better for you, should you know. (95)

Key Points

| From Ayah 91 to 95, several important expressions regarding the breaking of covenants and its punishment are observed, which themselves indicate the danger of this sin; the large number and power of you or others is not a justification or pretext for breaking covenants, «اُمة هى اربى». Allah tests you, «انما یبلوكم اللَّه». Allah will hold you accountable on the Day of Judgment, «لتسئلنّ». Breaking the covenant erases your steadfastness, «فتزلّ قدم». In this Ayah too, He says: Do not break the divine covenant for a small price.

Messages

1- The motive for breaking a covenant is attaining worldly gain. «لاتشتروا بعهداللَّه ثمناً قلیلاً»

2- Whatever you trade for, it is still little compared to breaking the covenant. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

3- Losing divine favors and breaking a covenant to achieve success is shortsightedness. «هو خیر لكم ان كنتم تعلمون»

Ayah

مَا عِندَكُمْ يَنفَدُ ۖ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ بَاقٍ ۗ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ صَبَرُوٓا۟ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۹٦)
That which is with you will be spent [and gone], but what is with Allah shall last, and We will surely pay the patient their reward by the best of what they used to do. (96)

Messages

1- Nothing and no one remains except Allah. «ما عندكم ینفد...»

2- Detachment from materialism, connection to Allah, steadfastness in commitments, and fulfilling promises require patience (sabr) and opposition to carnal desires. «و لنجزینّ الذین صبروا»

3- Detachment from the world is not deprivation; it is attainment. «و لنجزین الذین صبروا»

4- There is no better buyer than Allah, because He buys even ordinary or low-quality deeds at a high price. «بأحسن ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

مَنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُۥ حَيَوٰةً طَيِّبَةً ۖ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۹۷)
Whoever acts righteously, [whether] male or female, should he be faithful, We shall revive him with a good life and pay them their reward by the best of what they used to do. (97)

Key Points

"Hayat Tayyibah" (a good life) is when a person possesses a tranquil heart and a believing soul. The believer sees by the light of Allah, is included in the prayers of the angels, and is encompassed by Divine confirmations. Such individuals have no fear or grief.

Messages

1- The criterion for reward is not age, race, gender, social status, political position, etc. «مَن عَمِل»

2- Men and women are equal in attaining spiritual perfections. «من ذكر او انثى»

3- The condition for the acceptance of righteous deeds is faith. «و هو مؤمن»

4- Not even a single righteous deed goes unanswered. «من عمل صالحاً»

5- Wherever there is ignorant thinking (Jahili thought) based on belittling women, the name and status of women must be explicitly mentioned. «من ذكرٍ او انثى»

6- Good deeds alone are not sufficient; the person himself must also be good. «من عمل صالحاً... و هو مؤمن»

7- A person without faith and righteous deeds is as if dead. «فلنحیینّه حیوة طیّبةً»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ٱلرَّجِيمِ (۹۸)
When you recite the Quran, seek the protection of Allah against the outcast Satan. (98)
إِنَّهُۥ لَيْسَ لَهُۥ سُلْطَـٰنٌ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (۹۹)
Indeed he does not have any authority over those who have faith and put their trust in their Lord. (99)

Key Points

Every good deed may have afflictions (or pitfalls), just as honor has an affliction like arrogance, and serving people has an affliction like imposing obligation (or reminding them of the favor). Reciting the Quran may also have afflictions such as showing off, earning income, negative rivalries, deceiving people, misunderstanding, interpretation based on personal opinion (tafsir bi al-ra'y), and so on. Therefore, when reciting the Quran, a person must seek refuge with Allah from the evil of all these afflictions. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Recitation requires three things: a humble heart (qalb khashi'), a body free (from distraction), and an empty position (mawdi' khali'). Meaning, a state in which there is no prejudice. (73) If the Quran is accompanied by the interpretation and views of the infallible Imams علیهم السلام—"كتاب اللَّه و عترتى لن یفترقا" (The Book of Allah and My Progeny shall never separate)—and enters pure hearts—"هدىً للمتقین" (a guidance for the pious)—it becomes a cause for guidance, growth, and the increase of faith and knowledge. As the Quran says: O Prophet, whenever you recite the Quran, We place a barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter. (74) We read in narrations: The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite the phrase "اعوذ باللَّه من الشیطان الرجیم" (I seek refuge with Allah from the accursed Satan) when reciting the Quran. (75)

73) Tafsir Furqan. 74) Al-Isra, 45. 75) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- Do not be heedless of the danger of Satan's infiltration even among the best individuals and in the most sacred acts. (Reciting the Quran, even by a person like the Noble Prophet, requires seeking refuge.)

2- Satan approaches everyone, but he is not influential over everyone. "لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطَانٌ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا"

3- Faith and reliance (Tawakkul) are a fortress that protects a person from vulnerability. "لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطَانٌ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا"

4- Seeking refuge with Allah is a sign of faith in Him and reliance upon Him. "فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ ... آمَنُوا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ"

5- Your seeking refuge is accompanied by His granting of refuge. (So seek refuge, "فَاسْتَعِذْ," so that He may grant you refuge; if He does not grant refuge, our seeking refuge is futile.)

6- There is no other way except seeking refuge with Him. "فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ"

Ayah

إِنَّمَا سُلْطَـٰنُهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَوَلَّوْنَهُۥ وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِهِۦ مُشْرِكُونَ (۱٠٠)
His authority is only over those who befriend him and those who make him a partner [of Allah]. (100)

Key Points

Satan swore by the Honor of Allah that, with the help of his party and tribe, and through beautification (of evil), promises, propaganda, whisperings, and misguided individuals, he would hunt mankind from every direction. And people, in the face of this malicious intent, are divided into several groups, including:

  1. The Prophets, whose aspirations and goals Satan tries to obstruct, but he has no influence over the Prophets themselves. «القى الشیطان فى امنیّته» (The Devil interjects into his aspiration) means that Satan casts something into the goals and aspirations of the Prophet, but Allah neutralizes his plot. (76)
  2. The believers, whom Satan contacts, but they remember (Allah) and are saved. «اذا مسهم طائف من الشیطان تذكّروا» (When a visitation from Satan touches them, they remember [Allah]) (77)
  3. Ordinary people, into whose souls Satan penetrates and whispers. «یوسوس فى صدور الناس» (Who whispers into the breasts of mankind) (78)
  4. The wicked (Fasiq) individuals, into the depths of whose souls Satan enters and does not leave, gaining dominance over their thoughts, hearts, and limbs. «فهو له قرین» (He is to him an intimate companion) (79)

76) Hajj, 52. 77) Al-A'raf, 201. 78) An-Nas, 5. 79) Az-Zukhruf, 36.

Messages

1- We ourselves provide the prerequisites for Satan's dominance. «انّما سلطانه على الّذین یتولّونه» (Unless people accept the guardianship of the misguided, they cannot succeed.)

2- True monotheists are protected (insured), but whoever turns to other than Him becomes susceptible to influence. «انّما سلطانه على الّذین ... هم به مشركون» (His authority is only over those who... associate others with Him.)

Ayah

وَإِذَا بَدَّلْنَآ ءَايَةً مَّكَانَ ءَايَةٍ ۙ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُنَزِّلُ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُفْتَرٍۭ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱٠۱)
When We change a sign for another in its stead—and Allah knows best what He sends down—they say, ‘You are indeed a fabricator.’ Indeed, most of them do not know. (101)

Key Points

Sometimes a doctor changes the prescription because of the patient's changing condition, and this type of change also exists in the verses (Ayat) and rulings (Ahkam), which is called Naskh (abrogation). The opponents of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, as soon as they saw a command changed, would say: "You have mocked your companions, and every day you falsely fabricate a law and a command, and you cancel the previous law."

Messages

1- Religion has two types of laws: fixed and variable, and their change is in the hand of Allah. «بدلّنا»

2- All Divine laws have value in their own time and conditions. «آیةً مكان آیة»

3- People do not know the secrets of the Divine laws. «واللَّه اعلم ... اكثرهم لایعلمون»

4- The change in a Divine law is not a sign of doubt, regret, scientific or experiential growth, or weakness in legislation, but rather a sign of planning, wisdom, and attention to circumstances. «واللَّه اعلم»

5- The enemy takes advantage of every opportunity and uses everything as a pretext. «بدّلنا آیة مكان آیة قالوا...»

6- The root of some criticisms and accusations is ignorance. «بل اكثرهم لایعلمون»

Ayah

قُلْ نَزَّلَهُۥ رُوحُ ٱلْقُدُسِ مِن رَّبِّكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ لِيُثَبِّتَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَهُدًى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۱٠۲)
Say, the Holy Spirit has brought it down duly from your Lord to fortify those who have faith and as guidance and good news for the Muslims. (102)

Key Points

The meaning of Ruh al-Qudus (the Holy Spirit) is the same as Ruh al-Amin (the Trustworthy Spirit) and Gabriel, who has been named Ruh al-Qudus due to his purity from error and deviation.

Messages

1- Accusations must be answered. «قل نزّله روح القدس» (This is the response to the slander in the previous Ayah.)

2- The Divine verses were revealed gradually. «نزّله»

Regarding the revelation of the Quran, both the phrase «انزلنا» has been used, such as in the Ayah «انّا انزلناه فى لیلة القدر», and the phrase «نزّله». This is because the Quran has two revelations: once, instantaneously, upon the heart of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله on the Night of Qadr (Power), and once, gradually, over a period of twenty-three years.

3- The entire content of the Quran and the change of any law under its specific conditions is truth. «بالحقّ»

4- The revelation of the Divine message is among the affairs of Lordship and a necessity of Divine nurturing. «نزّله ... من ربّك»

5- The revelation of the Divine message is effective in the steadfastness of the Muslims. «نزّله، لیثبّت»

6- We should ask Allah for the success of steadfastness and the means for it. «نزّله ... لیثبّت»

7- What gives value to faith is steadfastness upon it. «لیثبّت»

8- The condition for receiving guidance and glad tidings is soundness of spirit and submission. «للمسلمین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّمَا يُعَلِّمُهُۥ بَشَرٌ ۗ لِّسَانُ ٱلَّذِى يُلْحِدُونَ إِلَيْهِ أَعْجَمِىٌّ وَهَـٰذَا لِسَانٌ عَرَبِىٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۱٠۳)
We certainly know that they say, ‘It is only a human that instructs him.’ The language of him to whom they refer is non-Arabic, while this is a clear Arabic language. (103)

Key Points

Apparently, a non-Arab person was living in Mecca, and the disbelievers used to accuse the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله of learning the teachings of the Quran from him and attributing them to Allah, while (the following points refute this):

  • How can two people who do not know each other's language teach each other?

  • How is it that at that time no one claimed, "I am the Prophet's teacher"?

  • How is it that the discourses revealed over a period of 23 years and under various circumstances have no contradiction with one another?

  • How is it that the teacher himself did not claim prophethood?

  • How is it that no one has yet answered the Quran's challenge and cry for contest—that if you bring even one Surah like it, I will retract My statement?

  • How could statements have been made during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) whose secrets scholars today have not even reached a fraction of?

  • How could a non-Arab have brought and taught the entirety of a Book, when all those opposing Arabs failed to produce even one Surah like it?

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ لَا يَهْدِيهِمُ ٱللَّهُ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱٠٤)
Indeed those who do not believe in the signs of Allah—Allah shall not guide them and there is a painful punishment for them. (104)

Key Points

Perhaps this Ayah is a continuation of the answer to the previous Ayah: if the intention behind teaching the Prophet of Islam is teaching language, how can that teacher, who is not an Arab, teach the Arab Prophet? But if the intention is the content of the Quran, then know that Allah does not guide the faithless group. Meaning, that teacher who does not believe in the divine verses has no access to all those lofty and true divine teachings of the Lord, let alone teaching them to others. (80)

And it is possible that this Ayah is a warning to those who fabricate a teacher for the Prophet, that Allah will not guide these individuals. 80) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يَفْتَرِى ٱلْكَذِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱٠٥)
Only those fabricate lies who do not believe in the signs of Allah, and it is they who are the liars. (105)

Key Points

In the two previous verses, the disbelievers, in the accusation they leveled against the Noble Prophet, claimed: A human taught him these verses, and he falsely attributes them to Allah. Allah replied that the teaching person is not an Arab. Furthermore, Allah would not grant all those profound teachings of the Quran to a disbeliever. And in this verse, He says: The Prophet is not someone who attributes the words of others to Allah; this is the action of those who lack faith. The Quran states: If they falsely attribute a word to Us, We will cut off their life vein.(81)

81) Al-Haqqah, 46.

Messages

1- The liar considers others to be liars as well. «انّما یفترى الكذب... هم الكاذبون»

Ayah

مَن كَفَرَ بِٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ إِيمَـٰنِهِۦٓ إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُۥ مُطْمَئِنٌّۢ بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ وَلَـٰكِن مَّن شَرَحَ بِٱلْكُفْرِ صَدْرًا فَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٠٦)
Excepting someone who is compelled [to recant his faith] while his heart is at rest in it, those who disbelieve in Allah after [affirming] their faith and open up their breasts to unfaith, Allah’s wrath shall be upon them and there is a great punishment for them. (106)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, the disbelievers of Mecca tortured and martyred the father and mother of Ammar ibn Yasir because they embraced Islam. When it was Ammar's turn to be tortured, he uttered the words the disbelievers wanted and saved his life. Ammar was criticized by some who thought he had abandoned Islam. Ammar came weeping to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and the Prophet stroked his head affectionately and said: If your life is ever in danger again, say these words and save yourself; you are full of faith from head to toe. This act is technically called Taqiyyah (dissimulation), which has specific rulings in Islam. However, we must know that the instances requiring Taqiyyah vary; sometimes Taqiyyah is obligatory, and sometimes one must stand firm until death, speak the truth, and not practice Taqiyyah. An example of this is the action of Pharaoh's sorcerers. As soon as they witnessed the miracle of Prophet Moses, they immediately believed, and they showed no fear in the face of Pharaoh's threats, telling him: Do whatever you wish, we will not abandon our faith. Pharaoh martyred them, and this resistance has been praised by the Quran. Of course, Taqiyyah is not a sign of apostasy, weakness, fear, retreat, defeatism, or surrender; rather, it is a type of camouflage and a tactic for preserving forces and programs. In narrations, Taqiyyah has been likened to a shield and an amulet.

Messages

1- The punishment for the apostate is Divine wrath in this world and the Hereafter. «من كفر باللَّه من بعد ایمانه ... غضب من اللَّه و لهم عذاب عظیم»

2- Man is exposed to the danger of having a bad end. «كفر باللَّه من بعد ایمانه»

3- Islam has made separate provisions for exceptional cases and necessities. «الاّ مَن اُكره»

4- A person's duty changes under duress. «الا من اُكره»

5- Confession and acknowledgment made under duress (and under torture and pressure) have no value. «من اُكره و قلبه...»

6- The spirit of Islam is faith in the heart. «و قلبه مطمئن»

7- Duress affects the body, not the heart. «اُكره و قلبه مطمئن»

8- The worst type of disbelief is separating oneself from the Muslims and opening one's heart to disbelief. «شرح بالكفر صدرا»

9- Faith is of two types: one that is intertwined with the human soul and is enduring, «قلبه مطمئن بالایمان», and one that is temporary or superficial. «كفر ... بعد ایمانه»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ ٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا عَلَى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٠۷)
That, because they preferred the life of the world to the Hereafter and that Allah does not guide the faithless lot. (107)

Messages

1- Worldliness and preferring it over the Hereafter is the ground for abandoning faith, apostasy, and incurring Divine wrath. «استحبّوا ...» (One of the causes of inclination towards materialism is the pursuit of comfort and escaping limitations. «استحبّوا الحیاة الدّنیا»)

2- Worldliness opens the human spirit and heart to accept disbelief (Kufr). «ذلك بانّهم»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ طَبَعَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰفِلُونَ (۱٠۸)
They are the ones Allah has set a seal on their hearts, their hearing and their sight, and it is they who are the heedless. (108)

Messages

1- Worldliness and giving it preference lead to heedlessness of the Hereafter, and result in not understanding, not hearing, and not seeing the realities (truths). (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۱٠۹)
Undoubtedly, they are the ones who will be the losers in the Hereafter. (109)

Messages

1- Whoever prefers the world over the Hereafter will suffer loss in the Hereafter and has wasted the capital of his growth. «هم الخاسرین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا فُتِنُوا۟ ثُمَّ جَـٰهَدُوا۟ وَصَبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۱٠)
Thereafter your Lord will indeed be forgiving and merciful to those who migrated after they were persecuted, waged jihad and remained steadfast. (110)

Key Points

By looking at the previous verses, we understand that Muslims were divided into several groups: 1. A group who died under the torture of the disbelievers and did not utter a single word of disbelief. Examples include the parents of Ammar Yasir. 2. A group who had faith in their hearts but practiced taqiyyah (dissimulation) to save their lives. Examples include Ammar. 3. A group who became disbelievers, or apostates (murtadd), after having believed. 4. A group who, after being afflicted by trial (fitnah) and deviation, returned to faith through migration (hijrah), struggle (jihad), and patience (sabr), repented, and preserved their religion.

The apostate (murtadd) is of two types: 1. The innate apostate (murtadd fitri) is someone born of Muslim parents who reverts to disbelief after accepting Islam. His punishment (due to the blow His apostasy inflicts upon the Islamic society and the destabilization of people's beliefs) is execution, confiscation of His property, and His repentance is not accepted. 2. The national apostate (murtadd milli) is someone whose parents were not Muslim at the time of His conception, but who became Muslim and later became a disbeliever. The repentance of this type of apostate is accepted.

Messages

1- Migration (hijrah) is valuable only if it is accompanied by struggle (jihad), patience (sabr), and steadfastness; otherwise, it is merely a form of escape. «هاجروا - جاهدوا - صبروا»

2- The condition for receiving divine forgiveness and mercy is struggle (jihad) and steadfastness. «هاجروا... جاهدوا... صبروا... من بعدها لغفور رحیم»

3- Islam has no dead ends, and every deviation and trial (fitnah) can be compensated for. (Even the apostate can believe and repent.) «ان ربّك من بعدها لغفور رحیم»

Ayah

۞ يَوْمَ تَأْتِى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ تُجَـٰدِلُ عَن نَّفْسِهَا وَتُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۱۱۱)
The day [will come] when every soul will come pleading for itself and every soul will be recompensed fully for what it has done, and they will not be wronged. (111)

Key Points

The scene of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) is so dangerous that the disbelievers, for their own salvation, say something every moment; sometimes they say: «واللَّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین» (82) (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists), sometimes they say: «هؤلاء اضلّونا» (83) (These people led us astray), and sometimes they say: «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین» (84) (If it were not for you, we would have been believers).

82) Al-An'am, 23. 83) Al-A'raf, 38. 84) Saba', 31.

Messages

1- The Resurrection (Qiyamah) is comprehensive, and all human beings will be present on that Day. «كلّ نفسٍ» (Every soul).

2- On the Day of Resurrection, man forgets everything and everyone, and is only concerned with his own salvation. «تجادل عن نفسها» (will argue on behalf of itself).

3- Our deeds in this world are not erased; rather, the deed and its effects remain. «توفّى... ما عملت» (will be paid in full... what it did).

4- The punishment of the Resurrection is the result of man's own actions, not oppression or vengeance. «ما عملت وهم لایظلمون» (what it did, and they will not be wronged).

Ayah

وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا قَرْيَةً كَانَتْ ءَامِنَةً مُّطْمَئِنَّةً يَأْتِيهَا رِزْقُهَا رَغَدًا مِّن كُلِّ مَكَانٍ فَكَفَرَتْ بِأَنْعُمِ ٱللَّهِ فَأَذَٰقَهَا ٱللَّهُ لِبَاسَ ٱلْجُوعِ وَٱلْخَوْفِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَصْنَعُونَ (۱۱۲)
Allah draws a parable: There was a town secure and peaceful, its provision coming abundantly from every place. But it was ungrateful toward Allah’s blessings. So Allah made it taste hunger and fear because of what they used to do. (112)

Key Points

The expression "garment" (لباس) used for hunger and fear is perhaps because these two, like a garment, had encompassed their entire existence and life. Similarly, the expression "to make taste" (چشاندن) is a metaphor for the penetration of the effects of hunger and fear deep into their being.

Messages

1- Human history possesses rules, laws, and is based on Divine traditions (Sunan). «ضرب اللَّه مثلاً»

2- We should take lessons from the Quranic examples regarding the history of past nations and peoples. «ضرب اللَّه مثلاً»

3- Security and tranquility are at the forefront of blessings and provide the foundation for the expansion of sustenance (rizq) and the economy of society. «آمنة مطمئنة یأتیها رزقها»

4- It is not necessary for a region to provide all its needs itself; rather, it can have imports. «یأتیها رزقها غداً من كلّ مكان»

5- Worldly punishments, compared to punishments in the Hereafter, are a type of tasting, not drinking. «فاذاقها اللَّه»

6- Ingratitude for blessings (kufrān al-ni'mah) incurs punishment right here in this world. «فكفرت ... فاذاقها اللَّه»

7- Ingratitude for blessings causes their removal (zawal). «فكفرت بانعم اللَّه فاذاقها اللَّه لباس الجوع»

8- Economic poverty and social insecurity are signs of a society's disregard for religious principles. «فكفرت ... فاذاقها اللَّه لباس الجوع و الخوف»

9- Ingratitude for blessings results in both physical punishment (poverty and hunger) and spiritual/psychological punishment (fear and insecurity). «فكفرت ... فاذاقها اللَّه لباس الجوع والخوف»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَهُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (۱۱۳)
Certainly there had come to them an apostle from among themselves, but they impugned him. So the punishment seized them while they were doing wrong. (113)

Key Points

This verse, following the previous one, states: The ungrateful people not only showed ingratitude for material blessings but also denied the greatest spiritual blessings, namely the Divine Prophets, and consequently, the wrath of Allah seized them.

Messages

1- The Prophets were from among the people and were human. The disbelievers knew them, but they showed obstinacy. «رسول منهم»

2- The punishment of Allah occurs after the completion of the proof (Hujjah). «جاءهم رسول ... فكذبوه فاخذهم العذاب»

3- Denying the Prophets is oppression against oneself, against the school (of thought), and against the leader. «فكذبوه... هم ظالمون»

Ayah

فَكُلُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ حَلَـٰلًا طَيِّبًا وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ (۱۱٤)
So eat out of what Allah has provided you as lawful and good, and give thanks for Allah’s blessing, if it is Him that you worship. (114)

Key Points

Islam permits the consumption of something only if it is both tayyib (pure/good) and halal (lawful). Things like wine (intoxicants) and pork are forbidden because they are inherently khabith (vile) and impure. And things purchased with usurped (illegally acquired) money, even if the item itself is tayyib, are forbidden because they are haram (unlawful). Now that ingratitude for blessings and the denial of the prophets lead to Allah's wrath and punishment, instead of ingratitude and disbelief (kufr), benefit from the lawful blessings, eat in a lawful manner, and be grateful.

Messages

1- There is no asceticism (riyadhat) or monasticism (rahbaniyyah) in Islam. «فكلوا مما رزقكم اللَّه»

2- Consumption is permissible within the framework of the lawful (halal), not just anything that is acquired. «فكلوا...حلالاً طیّباً»

3- The commands of religion are in accordance with human nature (fitrah). Foods that are agreeable to human disposition are permissible. «فكلوا... طیّباً»

4- A comprehensive religion has comprehensive commands. It considers both external purity (taharah) as a condition for consumption, «طیّبا» (pure), and internal/legal purity. «حلالاً» (lawful).

5- We should regard sustenance (rizq) as a divine blessing and gift, not merely the product of our own cleverness. «رزقكم اللَّه... نعمت اللَّه»

6- Eating is not for the sake of eating, but for fulfilling duties. «فكلوا... واشكروا... تعبدون»

7- Worship and servitude are incomplete without gratitude for blessings. «واشكروا نعمت اللَّه ان كنتم ایاه تعبدون»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَيْتَةَ وَٱلدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ ٱلْخِنزِيرِ وَمَآ أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ بِهِۦ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۱٥)
He has forbidden you only carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and that which has been offered to other than Allah. But if someone is compelled [to eat any of that], without being rebellious or aggressive, indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (115)

Key Points

This Ayah, with slight variations, also appears in Surahs Al-An'am and An-Nahl, which are Makki (Meccan), and Surahs Al-Baqarah and Al-Ma'idah, which are Madani (Medinan). "Ihlal" (اهلال) is derived from "Hilal" (هلال), meaning to raise the voice when sighting the crescent moon. Since the polytheists (mushrikun) would loudly pronounce the names of idols when slaughtering animals, this act is referred to as "Ihlal." (85) Although this Ayah mentions specific forbidden foods, it is not limited to them. Rather, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله specified other cases, which we must accept based on the Quran's own instruction: «ما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه و ما نهاكم عنه فانتهوا» (86)

85) Tafsir Namuneh. 86) Al-Hashr, 7.

Messages

1- The prohibition of anything belongs solely to Allah, and no one has the right to declare something lawful or unlawful based on their own initiative, superstitions, or delusions. «انّما حرّم»

2- The reason and philosophy behind the prohibition of certain foods are not solely hygienic and material issues; rather, avoiding spiritual impurities, such as polytheism (shirk), is also a criterion. «حرّم علیكم المیتة ... و ما اهلّ لغیراللَّه»

3- Even eating must be within the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid). «حرّم ... ما اهلّ لغیراللَّه»

4- Do not misuse the Divine law and its exceptions. «فمن اضطرّ غیر باغ و لا عاد»

5- Islam has no dead ends. In conditions of necessity ( اضطرار), the ruling changes. «فمن اضطرّ ... فانّ اللَّه غفور رحيم»

Ayahs

وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ لِمَا تَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُكُمُ ٱلْكَذِبَ هَـٰذَا حَلَـٰلٌ وَهَـٰذَا حَرَامٌ لِّتَفْتَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ (۱۱٦)
Do not say, asserting falsely with your tongues, ‘This is lawful, and that is unlawful,’ attributing lies to Allah. Indeed those who attribute lies to Allah will not be felicitous. (116)
مَتَـٰعٌ قَلِيلٌ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۱۷)
[Their share of the present life is] a trifling enjoyment, and there will be a painful punishment for them. (117)

Key Points

These two verses condemn any declaration of permissibility or prohibition that is not based on Divine law, considering it a lie fabricated against Allah in order to attain worthless worldly gain.

Messages

1- We should not express baseless or unwarranted opinions regarding what is lawful (halal) and unlawful (haram). (We must either be a Mujtahid ourselves or follow religious scholars.) «لاتقولوا ... هذا حلال وهذا حرام»

2- Innovation (Bid'ah) in religion and its rulings is forbidden. «ولاتقولوا ... هذا حلال و هذا حرامٌ»

3- The liar will not succeed. «ان الّذین یفترون ... لایفلحون»

4- The motive behind many innovations in religion is the attainment of worldly gain. «متاع قلیل»

5- Breaking the boundaries of lawful and unlawful brings both misery in this world and punishment in the Hereafter. «لایفلحون ... لهم عذاب الیم»

Ayah

وَعَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ حَرَّمْنَا مَا قَصَصْنَا عَلَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۱۱۸)
We forbade to the Jews what We have recounted to you earlier, and We did not wrong them, but they used to wrong themselves. (118)

Key Points

In a few verses prior, we read: «انّما حرّم» (He has only forbidden), meaning only four things are forbidden to you: carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and the animal slaughtered in the name of and for other than Allah. A question arises here: If forbidden foods are limited to these four things, then why were animals with claws (hooves) and the fat of cattle and sheep forbidden to the Jews in Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 146, which was revealed before An-Nahl?! Apparently, this verse answers by stating: What was forbidden to the Jews was done as an economic siege (embargo) and for their punishment and chastisement, not because it was truly forbidden (inherently unlawful). In Islam, there are two types of prohibition and restriction: one is a permanent prohibition for the general public, and the other is a temporary prohibition for a specific group. «حرّمنا ... كانوا انفسهم یظلمون» (We forbade... they were wronging themselves).

Messages

1- Economic siege through the prohibition of certain foodstuffs was one of the divine punishments and chastisements for previous nations. «حرّمنا... كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا۟ ٱلسُّوٓءَ بِجَهَـٰلَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوٓا۟ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۱۹)
Moreover, your Lord will indeed be forgiving and merciful to those who repent after they having committed evil out of ignorance and reform themselves. (119)

Key Points

Jahl (ignorance) means not knowing, but jahālah (foolishness/recklessness) is also used in cases where a person knows [the truth] but is overcome by whims and desires. In Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 54, "كتب ربّكم على نفسه الرّحمة انه من عمل منكم سوء بجهالة ثمّ تاب من بعده واصلح..." and also in [Surah] An-Nisa, Ayah 17, jahālah carries this meaning: "انّما التوبة على اللَّه للّذین یعملون السوء بجهالة ثمّ یتوبون من قریب..." Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Every sin committed by a servant, even if done intentionally, is called jahālah (foolishness), because the servant's ignorance compelled him to commit the sin and made the sin appear beautiful in his eyes.

Messages

1- Islam has no dead ends and has left the path of reconstruction open for humanity. "ثمّ تابوا"

2- Accepting repentance is among the functions of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and methods of nurturing. "ربّك"

3- True repentance is accompanied by reform and compensation for wrongdoings. "تابوا... واصلحوا"

4- The path of repentance is open to everyone. "للّذین"

5- Sins committed due to the dominance of desire, rather than denial or obstinacy, are close to being accepted [for repentance]. "عملوا السّوء بجهالةٍ ثمّ تابوا"

6- The acceptance of repentance by the Lord is certain. (Inferred from the use of Inna, la-Ghafūr, and the nominal sentence structure.)

7- In addition to accepting the wrongdoer, Allah is merciful towards him. "لغفور رحیم"

Ayah

إِنَّ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً قَانِتًا لِّلَّهِ حَنِيفًا وَلَمْ يَكُ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱۲٠)
Indeed Abraham was a nation [all by himself], obedient to Allah, a Hanif, and he was not a polytheist. (120)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Allah considered Abraham (Ibrahim) to be an Ummah (nation/community) by Himself, and each of the exegetes (mufassirun) has offered an explanation for analyzing and interpreting this phrase, including: 1. Because Abraham followed a school of thought (or path) that no one else followed, he was therefore an Ummah. (87) 2. He possessed goodness, virtue, and perfection equal to an Ummah, similar to the poetry that says: "What all the virtuous possess, you alone have." (88) 3. The word Ummah means a teacher of goodness; he was the teacher of virtues. (89) 4. The meaning of Ummah is the leader, the objective, and the Imam of all monotheists. (90) 5. His personality and the scope of his existence were equal to an Ummah. (91) 6. Because the stability (or foundation) of the Ummah depended on him. 7. Because he was knowledgeable, and a knowledgeable person guides an Ummah. 8. Because he created movement (or initiated action), he performed the work of an Ummah; he had no helper and rose up alone. (92)

87) Tafsir Safi. 88) Tafsir Maraghi. 89) Tafsir Tabari. 90) Tafsir Furqan. 91) Tafsir Namuneh. 92) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. Honoring the men of God and introducing the role models and heroes of Monotheism (Tawhid) is an educational method. «انّ ابراهیم»
  2. Number and quantity are not important; rather, quality and the creation of movement (or initiation of action) are important. «اُمّة»
  3. Do not fear loneliness on the path of truth. «انّ ابراهیم كان اُمّةً»
  4. Man is not condemned by his environment, society, or history. «و لم یَكُ من المشركین»
  5. Abraham was sincere both in action, «حنیفاً», and in belief. «و لم یك من المشركین»
  6. Conciliatory (or diplomatic) interactions do not harm a person's Monotheism. (Although Abraham, among the sun and star worshippers, showed tolerance and conciliation with them for moments, saying: "These are my Lord," but as soon as they disappeared, he said: "I do not love those that set." Despite this, in this Ayah, Allah says: He was not a polytheist for a single moment.) «لم یك من المشركین»
  7. What gives value to a person is conscious worship and obedience to Allah, «قانتاً», coupled with moderation and lack of deviation, «حنیفاً», and sincerity. «و لم یك من المشركین»

Ayahs

شَاكِرًا لِّأَنْعُمِهِ ۚ ٱجْتَبَىٰهُ وَهَدَىٰهُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۱۲۱)
Grateful [as he was] for His blessings, He chose him and guided him to a straight path. (121)
وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۲۲)
We gave him good in this world, and in the Hereafter he will indeed be among the Righteous. (122)

Key Points

The word ijtabā (اجتبى) comes from jabābah (جبابة), meaning collection and concentration. Allah's ijtibā (selection) means He protects individuals from dispersion and various inclinations, concentrating and purifying them for Himself. A good name, righteous progeny (dhūrrīyah ṭayyibah), the building of the Ka'bah, a long life, an answered prayer, and abundant wealth are examples of ḥasanah (goodness) in this world. Regarding "آتَيْنَاهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً" (We gave him goodness in the world), in these verses, Allah enumerates five perfections for Abraham and then grants him five stations (or ranks). As for the five perfections: (Ummah - Qānitā - Lam yakun min al-mushrikīn - Ḥanīfā - Shākirā). As for the five stations and favors from Allah: (Ijtabāh - Hadāh - Fī al-dunyā ḥasanah - Fī al-ākhirati la-min al-ṣāliḥīn - Ittaba'a Millata Ibrāhīm, which is in the next verse.)

Messages

1- Allah's selection is based on human merit. "قَانِتًا, حَنِيفًا, شَاكِرًا, اجْتَبَاهُ"

2- Whom Allah chooses, He guides to his destination. "اجْتَبَاهُ... هَدَاهُ إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ"

3- The prayers of the Prophets are answered. (Abraham had requested Allah to join him with the righteous: "رَبِّ هَبْ لِي حُكْمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ" (93). In this verse, Allah says: "إِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ" 93) Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 83.

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ أَنِ ٱتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱۲۳)
Thereafter We revealed to you [saying], ‘Follow the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, who was not a polytheist.’ (123)

Messages

1- Although the methods and circumstances of the prophets were not uniform, yet the path of the prophets is one, and they are commanded to follow the previous prophets. «اتّبع ملّة ابراهیم»

2- Successive praises using repeated expressions in adjacent verses are a sign of the perfect sincerity (ikhlas) of Ibrahim. (In three verses, «حنیفاً» and «ماكان من المشركین» have been repeated twice.)

Ayah

إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ ٱلسَّبْتُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ ۚ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۱۲٤)
The Sabbath was only prescribed for those who differed about it. Your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they differ. (124)

Key Points

The Jews were punished repeatedly by Allah due to their obstinacy and malice. One of these punishments was mentioned in Ayah 118 of this very Surah: «على الذین هادوا حرّمنا...» (For those who were Jews, We forbade...). The mandatory rest on Saturday (the Sabbath), which is mentioned in this Ayah, is another punishment. However, some groups showed gratitude while others were ungrateful, resorting to trickery, and consequently faced severe penalties. Considering the previous Ayah, perhaps the meaning of this Ayah is: O Prophet, follow the creed (Millah) of Abraham, for whom Friday was the day of rest. The mandatory rest on Saturday for the Jews was a type of penalty and punishment against them; otherwise, the true religion (A'in al-Haqq) is the rest on Friday, which has existed since the time of Abraham.

Messages

1- Allah's programs are sometimes based on mercy and sometimes based on wrath. «جعل السبت على الذین...»

Ayah

ٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِٱلْحِكْمَةِ وَٱلْمَوْعِظَةِ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ ۖ وَجَـٰدِلْهُم بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۖ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (۱۲٥)
Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good advice and dispute with them in a manner that is best. Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are guided. (125)

Key Points

From this Ayah until the end of the Surah, ten interesting moral instructions for interaction are presented. This Ayah gives a comprehensive instruction to all officials, educators, teachers, professors, and scholars, stating that for their success, they must be equipped with various methods concerning their diverse audiences, because not all people can be invited with a single method. Every person has a unique spirit and capacity, and one must speak to them in their own language; we should guide the elite with wisdom and argumentation, the common people with good admonition, and the opponents with the best disputation. Good admonition is that in which the preacher acts upon what he says, and good disputation is that which contains no insult or wrongful provocation of emotions.

Messages

1- The first duty of the Prophets is invitation (Da'wah). «اُدع»

2- The invitation must have a Divine direction. «الى سبیل ربّك»

3- Invitation has stages and levels. (Wisdom, admonition, and good disputation, where wisdom is the intellectual path and admonition is the emotional path.)

4- Admonition must be hasanah (good), but disputation must be ahsan (best/better). «الموعظة حسنة - بالتى هى اَحسن» (Both the content and the method/expression must be good.)

5- Explaining the effects and blessings of good deeds and the harms and dangers of bad deeds is among the methods of invitation. «بالحكمة» (Wisdom means familiarity with the benefits and detriments of affairs through knowledge and intellect.)

6- You are responsible for the duty, not for the result. «ان ربك هو اعلم بمن ضلّ عن سبیله و هو اعلم بالمهتدین» (Indeed, your Lord is best aware of those who stray from His path, and He is best aware of the guided ones.)

7- Wisdom and proof are always good, but admonition and disputation might be conducted with a good method or a bad method. (The words hasan and ahsan were not used for wisdom.)

8- Islam provides its followers with both intellectual nourishment, «بالحكمة», and spiritual nourishment, «الموعظة الحسنة», and deals logically with opponents. «جادلهم ...»

Ayah

وَإِنْ عَاقَبْتُمْ فَعَاقِبُوا۟ بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبْتُم بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَئِن صَبَرْتُمْ لَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لِّلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱۲٦)
Should you retaliate, retaliate with the like of what you have been made to suffer, but if you are patient that is surely better for the steadfast. (126)

Key Points

According to narrations, when Hadrat Hamza was martyred and mutilated (cut into pieces) in the Battle of Uhud, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: If we overcome the disbelievers, we will mutilate (cut into pieces) them. At this moment, this Ayah was revealed, [advising] that in the event of seeking revenge, do not lose sight of justice and patience. The Prophet said: I will be patient, I will be patient. Yes, to introduce Islam, we must compare the Battle of Uhud alongside the Conquest of Mecca and the pardon granted by the Messenger of Allah.

Messages

1- Observe justice and fairness even towards enemies and torturers. «بمثل ما عوقبتم»

2- Retaliation in kind (tit-for-tat) is a natural and divine right. «فعاقبوا»

3- There is a sweetness in patience that is not found in revenge. «و لئن صبرتم لهو خیرٌ»

4- Where excitement and disbelief are involved, present your promises with emphasis. (In «لَهو خیر» there is an emphasis that is not present in «فهو خیر»)

5- War also has laws and ethics. «بمثل ما عوقبتم , و لئن صبرتم ...»

6- Law alone is not effective; ethics are necessary. «بمثل ما عوقبتم» is law, and «لئن صبرتم» is ethics.

7- Do not think that your patience benefits the opponents; rather, it benefits yourselves. (A day will come when the green leaf of victory falls into your hands, the conscience of the opponents awakens, and the world, seeing the scenes of conflict between you, realizes your greatness and magnitude, and so on.) «فهو خیر للصّابرین»

Ayahs

وَٱصْبِرْ وَمَا صَبْرُكَ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا تَكُ فِى ضَيْقٍ مِّمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ (۱۲۷)
So be steadfast, and you cannot be steadfast except with Allah [’s help]. And do not grieve for them, nor be upset by their guile. (127)
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوا۟ وَّٱلَّذِينَ هُم مُّحْسِنُونَ (۱۲۸)
Indeed Allah is with those who are Godwary and those who are virtuous. (128)

Key Points

Inviting people through wisdom, good counsel, and beautiful argumentation is a very difficult task, because some groups show obstinacy and do not accept the invitation, while others, in addition to that, plot and obstruct the work, and yet others resort to overthrowing the system. To achieve their sinister goals, they use weapons such as slander, ridicule, threats, creating rivals, economic siege, war, and all forms of torture and harassment. Therefore, these two Ayahs (verses) are a form of consolation, reassurance, and guidance so that the Holy Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) does not reach a dead end in His invitation.

According to Tafsir Nemooneh (The Model Commentary), many blessings were mentioned in this Surah (chapter) to stimulate the spirit of gratitude. We list all forty blessings:

  1. Heaven/Skies: «خلق السموات»
  2. Earth: «والارض»
  3. Livestock/Cattle: «والانعام»
  4. Covering/Warmth: «لكم فیها دف ءٌ»
  5. Benefits of animals: «و منافع»
  6. Meat/Food: «منها تأكلون»
  7. Beauty and adornment: «فیها جمال»
  8. Transportation/Carrying burdens: «تحمل اثقالكم»
  9. Guidance: «على اللَّه قصد السبیل»
  10. Water/Drink: «منها شراب»
  11. Pastures/Grazing: «فیه تسیمون»
  12. Fruits: «و من كل الّثمرات»
  13. Night and Day: «سخرلكم الیل والنّهار»
  14. Sun and Moon: «والشّمس والقمر»
  15. Stars: «والنّجوم»
  16. Diverse blessings and creatures on Earth: «ذرأ لكم ما فى الارض مختلفاً الوانه»
  17. Sea and marine jewels: «سخر البحر ... تستخرجوا منه حلیة»
  18. Movement of ships: «ترى الفلك مواخر»
  19. Mountains: «والقى فى الارض رواسى»
  20. Rivers: «وانهاراً»
  21. Roads/Ways: «و سُبلاً»
  22. Natural signs/Landmarks: «و علاماتٍ»
  23. Finding direction through stars: «و بالنجم هم یهتدون»
  24. Reviving the dead earth: «فاحیى به الارض بعد موتها»
  25. Pure milk: «لبناً خالصاً»
  26. Fruit products (Intoxicants and good provision): «تتخذون منه سكراً و رزقاً حسناً»
  27. Honey/Cure: «فیه شفاء»
  28. Spouses: «من انفسكم ازواجاً»
  29. Children and grandchildren: «من ازواجكم بنین و حفدة»
  30. Provision/Good things: «رزقكم من الطّیبات»
  31. Hearing/Ears: «جعل لكم السّمع»
  32. Sight/Eyes: «والابصار»
  33. Intellect and spirit/Hearts: «والافئدة»
  34. Fixed dwelling/Homes: «من بیوتكم سكناً»
  35. Mobile dwelling/Tents from animal skins: «جعل لكم من جلود الانعام بیوتاً»
  36. Various types of clothing/Furnishings and goods from wool, fur, and hair: «من اصوافها و اوبارها و اشعارها اثاثاً و متاعاً»
  37. Blessing of shade: «جعل لكم مما خلق ظلالاً»
  38. Secure shelters in mountains: «من الجبال اَكناناً»
  39. Blessing of clothing that protects man from heat and cold: «سرابیل تقیكم ...»
  40. Blessing of armor and battle dress: «تقیكم بأسكم»

It is obvious that Allah’s purpose in mentioning these is not to confer favor (as a burden), gain prestige, or satisfy instincts, for He is exalted above such things. Rather, it is to create a spirit of gratitude (shukr), contemplation (tafakkur), submission (taslim), and remembrance (tadhakkur).

«لعلّكم تشكرون» Ayah, 14. «لعلّكم تهتدون» Ayah, 15. «لعلّهم یتفكّرون» Ayah, 44. «لعلّكم تسلمون» Ayah, 81. «لعلّكم تذكّرون» Ayah, 90.

Messages

  1. Patience (and every other success) comes from Him. «و ما صبرك الاّ باللَّه»

  2. The preacher must be patient. «ادع الى سبیل ربّك بالحكمة ... واصبر»

  3. Patience and victory are old friends; victory comes after patience. «واصبروا ...انّ اللَّه مع الذین اتّقوا»

  4. Faith in Divine assistance leads to broad-mindedness and patience. «واصبر ... ان اللَّه مع الذین»

  5. Piety (taqwa) and goodness (ihsan) are means of attracting Divine support. «انّ اللَّه مع الذین اتقوا والذین ... محسنون»
